_Actors and Singers matter.
Not because corporations say so --
but because people do._
Dear Valued Subscriber and Movie & Music Lovers,
As we step into another year celebrating authentic artistry, we at **[Heartland Journal](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/897c7536-10c7-485c-ac24-00b121546f09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** are proud to announce the **[2nd Annual Heartland AwardsTM 2025](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/6b30a897-aa86-41ed-904d-fda1d3358180?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** -- an awards celebration built by fans, for fans, and focused on the artists who truly matter.
The entertainment awards landscape has changed dramatically. MTV -- once the heartbeat of music and pop culture -- along with its companion channels, has faded from broadcast as Paramount Global pivots toward streaming.
The MTV Movie & TV Awards did not return in 2025. Even closer to home, the 2025 CMT Music Awards were cancelled amid corporate mergers and restructuring.
I mean did see the Grammys? Not great.
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/2903b8f4-9c84-4488-b654-87e994feb9e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As big corporations streamline schedules and chase profits, artists and fans are often left behind. When awards that once honored real talent disappear, something meaningful is lost.
But here's the good news: **[Heartland Journal is here-- and growing stronger.](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/897c7536-10c7-485c-ac24-00b121546f09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/33714960-83ef-483c-b6e0-7d77d1c999ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Unlike corporate-driven spectacles, the **[Heartland AwardsTM 2025](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/6b30a897-aa86-41ed-904d-fda1d3358180?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**put the power back where it belongs -- with the fans. Subscribers vote. Fans decide. No boardrooms. No sponsor agendas.
This is about honoring artists whose work moves us, reflects our communities, and carries the heartland spirit across music, film, and beyond -- regardless of charts, streams, or corporate backing.
Learn more about categories, voting, and what's ahead at **[HeartlandJournal.com](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/6b30a897-aa86-41ed-904d-fda1d3358180?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
* Vote for your favorite actors and singers
* Cast your ballot when voting opens -- every vote counts equally
* Celebrate winners chosen purely by fans

This is more than an awards show.
It's a reminder that art's true value comes from connection -- not revenue.
Together, we're keeping the spirit of the heartland alive in movies and music.
**Steve Abramowicz**
Editor-in-Chief
Heartland Journal
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/9840939c-9fa9-4716-a0ff-aa9bf3a139d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**[Proud Hosts of the 2nd Annual Heartland AwardsTM](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/6b30a897-aa86-41ed-904d-fda1d3358180?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

[Voting opens January 30th and closes March 31st at midnight.](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/897c7536-10c7-485c-ac24-00b121546f09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Winners announced in April.
P.S. Leaderboards and voting details are coming soon -- exclusively for subscribers.

| Monetary Metals So now you own gold. It was a great decision, one rooted in freedom, independence, and protecting your purchasing power from the chaos of fiat. But until the day you need it, what’s your gold doing? If it’s just sitting there, vaulted or buried, it’s not helping you today. And in a world of rising costs, endless printing, and financial overreach... that’s a missed opportunity. Here’s the question most gold owners never ask:
**Why not put your gold to work?** That’s the opportunity [**Monetary Metals®**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) unlocks. Monetary Metals built an innovative gold leasing platform that allows gold owners, like you to [**earn up to 4% annually, paid in gold**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D), without selling or giving up ownership. ✔ Your gold is leased to vetted businesses that use it productively
✔ You earn passive income, paid directly in more gold No fiat. No speculation. No Wall Street shell games.
Just sound money doing what sound money _should_ do: serve the people who own it. [Monetary Metals](https://click.parlermailer.com/?%3D%3D) has already helped thousands of gold holders turn passive metal into productive income, all while aligning with the same principles that led them to buy gold in the first place: **personal responsibility, freedom, and independence from a broken system.** Owning gold is a hedge.

name: hear.com
subject: Hearing aids just got their biggest upgrade
body:
Why this tiny German hearing aid is taking the U.S. by storm, according to the experts

| [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zs54rhj6dc07j2n1ld3ifgapjekc6lh5tvekrd88_rp22sh2s8i6epr0chh60o32cth2bs)
---
| Why this tiny German hearing aid is taking the U.S. by storm, according to the experts
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z76l2d012kbjuaina8j2ofga6d6kvfhqrvf50pv8_rp22sh2s8i6epr0chh60o32cth2bs)
**Alarming fact: More than 48 million Americans hear so poorly that their quality of life significantly suffers as a result.**

The problem: Most wait too long to act, hoping their hearing will improve on its own. Sadly, it never does.

But now, **a game-changing device is revolutionizing the hearing aid industry,** and experts say it's the biggest breakthrough they've seen in over a decade.

It's hear.com's **new IX hearing aid.**

And it’s the perfect solution at the perfect time—when more and more people are eager to maintain their quality of life and overcome these limitations.

It’s no wonder **hear.com's IX hearing aid has taken the U.S. by storm since its release earlier this year** offering hope and unprecedented clarity to thousands across America.
[Check if you qualify](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z1ujss049btir32v2227rlbnsj5bk712h0pqcvn0_rp22sh2s8i6epr0chh60o32cth2bs)
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z1b5fdoumcj1mj0hi157e7ohtcu25kjs8s1tbf80_rp22sh2s8i6epr0chh60o32cth2bs)
What makes hear.com’s IX hearing aid so special?

It’s one of **the world’s first hearing aids with dual processing.**

This is special because it’s the first time engineers have been able to pack not one, but two state-of-the-art computer chips into a device this small.

The result? **An unprecedented amount of power and clarity.**

This means that hear.com’s IX hearing aids offer all of the usual benefits of modern hearing aids—from smartphone connectivity to Bluetooth streaming—with the added bonus of something completely new: they can process speech and background noise independently, then recombine them for **unparalleled, crystal clear sound.**

“It's not just about volume,” explains hear.com founder Paul Crusius. “It's about speech clarity. And that's precisely what IX is designed to optimize.”

In other words: **with double the power comes double the clarity—and all that in a virtually invisible design!**

Limited no-risk trials now available

Click below to check if you qualify for a 45-day no-risk trial. As the Germans always say: "there's never been a better time." So do yourself a favor and don’t wait. **Get started today.**
[Check if you qualify](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zfh0objpkc8p11u9liaq1a9f4e5rs91pji1lagso_rp22sh2s8i6epr0chh60o32cth2bs)

name: Tactical
subject: Putting a stunner and a stinger in your mailbox
body:
| | | |
---
---
|
---

body:
Accusing Jews of conducting recon tours and spying on the Capitol is par for the course for Boebert.
| | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However [if you'd like to unsubscribe,](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001xr3SR24zm5z7miygtmvPykEDrU9uLCmOaH8cFpbibu2m3YXRHF_utFHoEV2S66JygHo9oxIk1KUcgoNbZK7DcTqyXOhhiD_ZGw-SkoCpRXsb506prxpAV58IMfE1e8RaKKZupsypaEutqoHXpVeeqFAqCY5lG9kriioqIFlw9RFPzm5KcRZK1LQCB1MezH_pgndPpM01sqk=&c=um6HTPXDYoII91MhcXtyUa7tcwn1dwvl5fO_slVKsDYS3mpg7T_Qig==&ch=67OPB1-dAubQJkjkSqdrHqi1rvZp3BxJEgcJGcpBMUgWUK2glwllZA==) you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email.

| Peter,
This year marked the five year anniversary of the January 6th attack on our nation's Capitol.
Congresswoman Lauren Boebert was not a bystander, she played a key role in setting the stage for the attack. In the days leading up to the insurrection, she led groups of MAGA militia on "tours" of the Capitol, pointing out entrances and security weak points.
---
| [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001xr3SR24zm5z7miygtmvPykEDrU9uLCmOaH8cFpbibu2m3YXRHF_utCVfvV-XxMf3vNBBwETq_yLYBjy0WCUulEcL4bGrffdnlOr4-vEhiJ0aMuuLCFtblRBaFfj5POmRAb43iP9hQ_3iM_CiQl7s8BBBmgPhyDW1gnKf41qVjXIDFttoSPCARrZOPO8ECJF1YHUtaFPDez1n21uiquyMq91cxigYuWQVr_MKbTg0vps=&c=um6HTPXDYoII91MhcXtyUa7tcwn1dwvl5fO_slVKsDYS3mpg7T_Qig==&ch=67OPB1-dAubQJkjkSqdrHqi1rvZp3BxJEgcJGcpBMUgWUK2glwllZA==)
---
Boebert never faced accountability. She even voted against honoring the brave Capitol Police officers. And later, she mocked a group of Jewish visitors in the Capitol, asking them if they were "doing reconnaissance" -- a callback to how the media framed what she did in the leadup to Jan. 6.
---
| [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001xr3SR24zm5z7miygtmvPykEDrU9uLCmOaH8cFpbibu2m3YXRHF_utCVfvV-XxMf3vNBBwETq_yLYBjy0WCUulEcL4bGrffdnlOr4-vEhiJ0aMuuLCFtblRBaFfj5POmRAb43iP9hQ_3iM_CiQl7s8BBBmgPhyDW1gnKf41qVjXIDFttoSPCARrZOPO8ECJF1YHUtaFPDez1n21uiquyMq7CsZATT2liPLPISvyfmT7Q=&c=um6HTPXDYoII91MhcXtyUa7tcwn1dwvl5fO_slVKsDYS3mpg7T_Qig==&ch=67OPB1-dAubQJkjkSqdrHqi1rvZp3BxJEgcJGcpBMUgWUK2glwllZA==)
---
In 2022, while walking through the House of Representatives, Lauren Boebert encountered a large group of Jewish tourists that included Rabbis and Orthodox Jews with traditional beards and yarmulkes. She came up to the group and asked them if they were "doing reconnaissance."
This behavior isn't new. Boebert and her allies thrive on spreading hate and conspiracies especially about Jews. Take for instance the conspiracy propagated by Boebert co-conspirator Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene that "Jewish space lasers" were causing wildfires across America.
Accusing Jews of conducting recon tours and spying on the Capitol is par for the course for Boebert. In her five years on Capitol hill, her callous hate fueled speech has alienated Palestinians, Jews, Muslims, the disabled, Ranchers, the list goes on and on.
This behavior caught up to her and forced her to flee her old district where her constituents had turned against her. Now, the constituents in her new district are turning on her too and she is more vulnerable than ever.
Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, as her Democratic opponent, labor leader Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts as Boebert underperformed Trump in every single county.
Now Trisha is back to finish the job and she needs your help. [If there was ever an election to beat her, it is this one. Can we count on you to make a donation of $25, $15, or even $5 to help Team Trisha remove Boebert once and for all?](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001xr3SR24zm5z7miygtmvPykEDrU9uLCmOaH8cFpbibu2m3YXRHF_utCVfvV-XxMf3Qx3WjGUPBfqur0lv2KFqDft6c_fT1hSD_cGuV7cb9je-wAAeRqFeavKrmiENH1SJfGPn4gxt0kXj6wr0pMQb4-8zV0iEZMQPtJKujsAN1hAqJKvpwWZ1R6Wrjvj2O3pPD72ofrPdD1etulC01bS721Af2KXxdEs-RHQHNBRX6VpVGvjOucygAA==&c=um6HTPXDYoII91MhcXtyUa7tcwn1dwvl5fO_slVKsDYS3mpg7T_Qig==&ch=67OPB1-dAubQJkjkSqdrHqi1rvZp3BxJEgcJGcpBMUgWUK2glwllZA==)

Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However == you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email. | | == | Peter, This year marked the five year anniversary of the January 6th attack on our nation's Capitol. Congresswoman Lauren Boebert was not a bystander, she played a key role in setting the stage for the attack. In the days leading up to the insurrection, she led groups of MAGA militia on "tours" of the Capitol, pointing out entrances and security weak points. | == Boebert never faced accountability. She even voted against honoring the brave Capitol Police officers. And later, she mocked a group of Jewish visitors in the Capitol, asking them if they were "doing reconnaissance" -- a callback to how the media framed what she did in the leadup to Jan. 6. | == In 2022, while walking through the House of Representatives, Lauren Boebert encountered a large group of Jewish tourists that included Rabbis and Orthodox Jews with traditional beards and yarmulkes. She came up to the group and asked them if they were "doing reconnaissance." This behavior isn't new. Boebert and her allies thrive on spreading hate and conspiracies especially about Jews. Take for instance the conspiracy propagated by Boebert co-conspirator Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene that "Jewish space lasers" were causing wildfires across America. Accusing Jews of conducting recon tours and spying on the Capitol is par for the course for Boebert. In her five years on Capitol hill, her callous hate fueled speech has alienated Palestinians, Jews, Muslims, the disabled, Ranchers, the list goes on and on. This behavior caught up to her and forced her to flee her old district where her constituents had turned against her. Now, the constituents in her new district are turning on her too and she is more vulnerable than ever. Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024, as her Democratic opponent, labor leader Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts as Boebert underperformed Trump in every single county. | | Friends,
Michigan is winnable in 2026, but only if Republicans nominate candidates who can win statewide and govern competently once elected.
In a competitive state like Michigan, down-ballot races such as Attorney General and Secretary of State can make or break the entire ticket. In elections like this, experience, electability, and institutional knowledge of the Democratic opposition matter. Strong candidates lift the ticket. Weak ones can drag it down, as outlined in a recent analysis on electability: [The RIGHT Side News](https://jrhsf9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_D3y02By3zBgM1Al3OR838sqOFZ729xpa03KroRAHdb71A_YOfdg6u_4kF5_iSIS1Pd-E9m10ACmOJMcl6A3gNe2rJDvnqvyV_xcLg30djV1l5B7QNmRhkW9OlK5zIzkPVz6zNPFFybTOa_PmcLMw14otCmI9aw6K5cc6s1CoCjCJH_YbxNuUSiHFXR2Ikptyfiw_ERZFuHP0I-JwgtFggta_PapUhq5m7BAA85NdzT4yAot7zQevuC4Dy6VP19MRPrJOrXR4M8=&c=mUcw3Q213njdVmL1yIW5sZgqjKR3fa6CJmqWC2S26EvWtlz2-sBVNw==&ch=yXcPiTlkXDo9tc0pd-89RvG2cgzkIxRraK3nIOFXTAHToLdKBuodzw==)
I am running for Attorney General because I bring the professionally relevant experience, judgment, and proven leadership required for the job. The Attorney General's office is not a training ground. It is a constitutional office with broad authority that demands a deep understanding of law enforcement, government, and the limits of power.
That experience matters now more than ever because the likely Democratic nominee, Oakland County Prosecutor Karen McDonald, is not a symbolic candidate. She has aligned herself with the activist approach of Dana Nessel, Gretchen Whitmer, and Jocelyn Benson. Her public praise for Nessel's lawsuits against President Trump makes clear that the same political activism would continue, this time with greater reach and sophistication.
That is what makes this race dangerous. A progressive Democrat with prosecutorial credentials and an activist mindset can do real damage from the Attorney General's office.
Michigan cannot afford Dana Nessel 2.0.
Republicans must nominate a candidate who can credibly contrast that record with respect for the rule of law, public safety, and constitutional limits. I bring experience directly aligned with the duties of the Attorney General, a record of enforcing the law without politicizing it, and the credibility to win independents and moderate Democrats who decide statewide elections.
I ask delegates to look beyond campaign branding and slogans. This race is about whether we nominate a prepared, proven candidate who strengthens the ticket, or someone whose lack of readiness or personal vulnerabilities become a liability Democrats will exploit.
Voters want competence. They want experience. And they want an Attorney General who will protect their rights, improve public safety, and keep law enforcement out of partisan politics.
I entered this race to win and to restore the Michigan Attorney General's office to its proper role. With your support, we can lift the ticket, defeat the Democrats in November, and put Michigan back on the right track.
Join me. Together, we can win.
Sincerely,
Doug Lloyd Eaton County Prosecuting Attorney Candidate for Michigan Attorney General

clean_body: Strong Leadership Lifts the Ticket | | | | | == | | | == | | Friends, Michigan is winnable in 2026, but only if Republicans nominate candidates who can win statewide and govern competently once elected. In a competitive state like Michigan, down-ballot races such as Attorney General and Secretary of State can make or break the entire ticket. In elections like this, experience, electability, and institutional knowledge of the Democratic opposition matter. Strong candidates lift the ticket. Weak ones can drag it down, as outlined in a recent analysis on electability: == I am running for Attorney General because I bring the professionally relevant experience, judgment, and proven leadership required for the job. The Attorney General's office is not a training ground. It is a constitutional office with broad authority that demands a deep understanding of law enforcement, government, and the limits of power. That experience matters now more than ever because the likely Democratic nominee, Oakland County Prosecutor Karen McDonald, is not a symbolic candidate. She has aligned herself with the activist approach of Dana Nessel, Gretchen Whitmer, and Jocelyn Benson. Her public praise for Nessel's lawsuits against President Trump makes clear that the same political activism would continue, this time with greater reach and sophistication. That is what makes this race dangerous. A progressive Democrat with prosecutorial credentials and an activist mindset can do real damage from the Attorney General's office. Michigan cannot afford Dana Nessel 2.0. Republicans must nominate a candidate who can credibly contrast that record with respect for the rule of law, public safety, and constitutional limits. I bring experience directly aligned with the duties of the Attorney General, a record of enforcing the law without politicizing it, and the credibility to win independents and moderate Democrats who decide statewide elections. I ask delegates to look beyond campaign branding and slogans. This race is about whether we nominate a prepared, proven candidate who strengthens the ticket, or someone whose lack of readiness or personal vulnerabilities become a liability Democrats will exploit. Voters want competence. They want experience. And they want an Attorney General who will protect their rights, improve public safety, and keep law enforcement out of partisan politics. I entered this race to win and to restore the Michigan Attorney General's office to its proper role. With your support, we can lift the ticket, defeat the Democrats in November, and put Michigan back on the right track. Join me. Together, we can win. Sincerely, Doug Lloyd Eaton County Prosecuting Attorney Candidate for Michigan Attorney General 👉 == | | | | | == == == == == == == == == == | | | | == == == == | | | | | == | | | | Doug Lloyd for MI Attorney General | PO Box, 80536 | Lansing, MI 48906 US | | | | | | | |

And in a world of rising costs, endless printing, and financial overreach... that’s a missed opportunity. Here’s the question most gold owners never ask:

Just sound money doing what sound money _should_ do: serve the people who own it. [Monetary Metals](https://click.parlermailer.com/?) has already helped thousands of gold holders turn passive metal into productive income, all while aligning with the same principles that led them to buy gold in the first place: **personal responsibility, freedom, and independence from a broken system.** Owning gold is a hedge.

name: Eddie Seay
subject: January Showed Up and Showed Out!
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| Friends--
Mid-December and January didn't just show up -- we showed OUT. As if preparing for the General Assembly wasn't already a full-throttle sprint, Freedom Virginia kicked it into overdrive with a massive, statewide push on affordability.
It all started in Virginia Beach, with protests, office visits, and loud, clear demands for accountability from Rep. Jen Kiggans in the 2nd District. We took on the threats to ACA tax credits and SNAP benefits head-on -- and people answered the call. The rallies were electric. The energy was real. The turnout was undeniable.
---
| |
---
Then we hit the road. From Yorktown to Manassas, our partner driven Affordability Summits brought hundreds of Virginians face-to-face with state legislators for powerful, informative conversations about what families actually need to survive and thrive.
---
| |
---
| We closed it out the same way we started -- big and bold -- with a visibility rally in Virginia Beach that once again drew hundreds in support of extending critical tax credits and an Affordable Medicine Advocacy Day.
---
| |
---
This momentum doesn't happen by accident. It happens because of your hard work, your voices, and your commitment. And we're not slowing down. Freedom Virginia will keep advocating, organizing, and creating safe, welcoming spaces for all people -- because this fight is far from over, and we're in it together.
---
| |
---
[](https://dpxqxvhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DsH-3QJBLXe4swip0nrKB0d7-dhh4cnNlpCCdQfLumsQTMdSo68VFXMaTGYwf2rycShNYN9DpQK9Gk7ahqh9l4SJ0F818oi3Mch7OETUlqn16T4Ob1_PNAnZg_027a7fQTGofzkyogCeuOBTeAbRGH_PxbGQOIMOOwBvWBgI2X4=&c=cjCXaOXhKtFdj8yNNRTkdOTMMjT-MDd3ajwJtwVgrygmVkyfo7fs2g==&ch=NLy51GocBD4hFVUrxm-h5yh61vxuWo4n5H4b9Wpr1JudY11vIV8gQQ==) [](https://dpxqxvhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DsH-3QJBLXe4swip0nrKB0d7-dhh4cnNlpCCdQfLumsQTMdSo68VFXMaTGYwf2rykDEQJnT1KSLKBeWoNbxgpsyTvHcmQQ77NH21PRx4vSbt0wLRGKCdgkLQZnAQTWA9gdXrMJNrC8IO36WPs4UM64T5_4VJpHHR&c=cjCXaOXhKtFdj8yNNRTkdOTMMjT-MDd3ajwJtwVgrygmVkyfo7fs2g==&ch=NLy51GocBD4hFVUrxm-h5yh61vxuWo4n5H4b9Wpr1JudY11vIV8gQQ==) [](https://dpxqxvhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DsH-3QJBLXe4swip0nrKB0d7-dhh4cnNlpCCdQfLumsQTMdSo68VFXMaTGYwf2ryHQhayH2stRfXXAdt7s2m2L90jB22j4eAzQVKx03DnluLGgaB4W1qTVU2_-cPE-xNty0LZamJM7FV9dGG7mdRSHF1LV-q1aAtLZHUkkEhI4A=&c=cjCXaOXhKtFdj8yNNRTkdOTMMjT-MDd3ajwJtwVgrygmVkyfo7fs2g==&ch=NLy51GocBD4hFVUrxm-h5yh61vxuWo4n5H4b9Wpr1JudY11vIV8gQQ==)

name: Rhena Hicks
subject: 💞Love Is In the Air 💞
body:
February is upon us, and the first few weeks of the 2026 Virginia General Assembly Session have flown by!
| | |
---
| |

| Friends-
February is upon us, and the first few weeks of the 2026 Virginia General Assembly Session have flown by! Love is definitely in the air this month, and we're sharing our love for affordability all around the legislature. Here are a few ways you can help spread the love this month:
💞Contribute! name: The Flyover
subject: Project Vault, Most Reliable Cars, and Musical Chocolate Bars
body:

# Tuesday, February 3, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1863, writer Samuel Clemens [first used the pen name Mark Twain](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGupCAYBeCnwQ6DoDIWFNvYzbZbGoRf_WcRDPyj8e1v5ua-wa2_nJOT8-85PS0GjOtfu4OhJU0RrhKACPJ05PQCR6d9B9pToQwB7RzATVLIngvJhZq8xXBPbeXNAiBBVWAarUXT9Lofqs34bnj4BoTr5scsndfS9X5oh06o-eFaqNB82oQUqmlEL3Tdia5rvdKL0taBXVgrwCOlXL8SRtpgCXc6Idcu7VUwG9FRmPrD5MjkeF1XvWGhlO-PMznShoV7e3OM_EeYHEeY89vmmysmx93m_5wui5HPsGIsPMORMmFcP6kT84oRLXdId5WNPy4MAUuvWSvW3WL4nkK_-u808isAAP__lVWHhg) in stories for a Virginia City newspaper. He borrowed the name from his riverboating days, when the call meant the water was two fathoms deep. New Consumer Reports data show which car brands are expected to be the [most reliable in 2026](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL8GMiNjA54JHFxCNpdeiBq7PfScwcjuAc3fr1hpv2DzUlW99-8xPYAibc-_sKLlkKYNzxKRGfO05_RCxwe8I6-pcMZIMEd0k5JqEFIJqScPFD9TV3sbEBXqGm1rjGzbwQz3erGh06FFZ3DuAd0N_Gzc3esBMXTa32RN9mqTSuq2lYM0TS_7vvPaBG3AIYSqk-iJU25eiTZeMMRPOjA3Lq11tAvzXir9p1JjpcbzPJuDyhuig50YIhW-gpUaM2z_0QteUFw04gdHOMhizrD5ImgT15tKjXW2fj8pRiqDqTr5XIHi9yT_ytNh1VcAAAD___9wfrA), with Japanese manufacturers leading the rankings, as we report below. A young woman has been awarded millions by a New York jury after suing over a double mastectomy she received as a minor during a gender transition. The verdict, the first of its kind to reach trial, raises questions about medical standards of care. Host Ayla Brown explains on today’s episode of The Flyover Podcast. [Tune in here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykz7FugzAQgOGngS3ofDa4GTx0yZauHdGBz4mjAyNzTcrbV-0r9AH-X_o-r-OVsuT19kELB01lXPm1C6tyHbdaHjzrk75El7JrZck0Cc8jAg4nwBPYMVKWY3RtDIkZ2bYcjPdgzOCHc3sPDAmc7WfjJiLLb-aMEaxDmnByFn2bw-8NEKwxMIDveuh7F61P1tPMlBoHHLOW2j1KXvXOSY7y5NrNZWkl3FW3vbHvDV4avFSeKq2xk6PBy1Yif2sl09YQt1cWyfvgGwe3hbL89fov9DPgTwAAAP__NI1rHg). From today’s sponsor, [Frontieras](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O2zAMgJ_G3hLQkiw5g4cCh2zXtaNAW3TCg34Mic7h3r7wtegLdOX3gSS-X-_-HTlyfvzERLNsxWf6bJFEqPq9lg9a5YVHlFSaVIqMS6TVK1D2AuoC2gfk-OVNH-aNSJHuaR6cg2Gwzt7656yn2xK0VmincTFqMOtkrAIcw7KMLkDP87kNFOhhAAvuOsI4mqDdph2uhFtngAJLqdePwlmetMWv8qJ6XUvq4_wU2Vunf3Tq3qk75xc1uW61ZGGq2E7rRPp-SPKtHHWlTr9RQo6dsucwUeAjdfptRw6XHatkqu3J-1--YtqRH_nb-AeHCS561P5PhiOzoDdwmxQYZ93Q1znsnxwjN-s6A4_z4PfL8l-dX7P6HQAA__-pspHs): See why some investors say this patented coal-to-commodities breakthrough could be a rare “Rockefeller Moment” as the company works toward a potential NASDAQ listing.

## Trump Launches $12B Critical Minerals Stockpile
President Trump [launched a project](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQAL8Gd0R4MZAUFNe4y7VXWgQvMbk1WHjjyH9_yv3Cq0eakebPfbqHTLk8f4cVPac6FfzshMzYpq3VF0Y-wpt4rTs3pBwehHECBVYqkEpPc8h0TkM3-4QIqDv0vXOq762zt27xKcBwjWa-ag3xocGmBOEWzWAfShujuuy_NgVK972yyl2MMmaYtUvahYghiUHhnLm2y6vmwgsmOuuB7RLr2pFfmLdd6F8CRgEjL7hkoi8TMG6VcjwFjFiwPU-J5citlhULCxiN66-2d5Lbe93kmgu2QLvcuca_WyaULTSUGBovAsau-Xn7ZKK8WycG9VxD_t_p-EfjDg__AgAA___dLYNJ) to amass a $12 billion stockpile of critical and rare-earth minerals, dubbed Project Vault, to slash U.S. reliance on Chinese materials and curb Beijing's leverage in trade talks. The stockpile will be [funded by a $10 billion loan](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ8GOiNjbAiFi7-hy99eiYy9JJtbbGRvwuXtT-QVrh19Gs18X9f56pAw3v67DSyvaY5wFAJmyPOe0wM8v9yTeEuFMxC6hcDPSqpeSCVkNweH9J51HewKoKCrwbbDINu2H_qxvtvLOqqgR70sQRozankxWo2-NdqMy2i6Gu3ZJpXs2lb2cmiMNEaHbli7wXlwa6UlBOSUm0fCyHdY6Z1ekBuftprsnXkvVfevUlOlpuM4mjX9LM-CEUo5mUpNeyJk9KVSE-fntosFbhhF4eS_989_4fNJOBIbRsiOimiVWJAIUxQFIIgtRXjX2Yb9OOPSD5WWt80hfZbwn_S9rPoNAAD__2Idhs0) from the U.S. Export-Import Bank and nearly $2 billion in private capital, with minerals procured and stored for automakers, the defense industry, tech firms, and other manufacturers. China currently controls between 60% and 70% of the rare-earth market, which provides foundational materials for modern technology ranging from batteries to MRI machines to F-35 fighter jets. The U.S. was entirely reliant on imports for [12 critical minerals in 2024](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ_GdEbLmmClcPE3dPnbK9HGXoJzBiN7E5S3P3GvcN2MRprRfF-36UYxxe3xn1Z2Mudp46MmFuEy7SU_2cubXknWXKVwinRP7CcEHDSgBjMFiukz9U1wMzOyadh11kLXDXa4Novr-p4MgvHXYJCMDxQAiKwZ7HwlH5rozjZAMF0HA9j2ApdLH4ydjSXPNKseOETJpX3muMnCc_rkN5fW57VJbhHZqzL_FI4Kx-M4Wn-v54szVzieUuEo5bXuulBhzVRk0WvcuFCqukr233tMrDvU95hSzJvCsSku7Mdp62BVD4-VYvrdlD-Bejv8CQAA__-TRn6O) and imported 50% or more of 29 others, according to the White House.
## Gloucester Boating Tragedy Claims Seven
The U.S. Coast Guard on Monday [identified all seven victims](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuOpDAURL_GZG75ARgCB5uQzSaj1YbIj2u4I4Nb9h1a_fcr9hcmqqCkUp3z92P9cJjx3H67Ayylsp7wahmIoK7PWr4g0OW-Mx2lUYWMzmcIqxJq5EJxodfoML_Xvos2ASjQHVhpjJByNOPc7VYKPYATyk9aRkg6jWEclI_DPAc_S9-hvdeEElpKMQrzGMQw9FGbpI0L4BLrBUSkUh9fBU_aIeV3uaA-Qjm6bHeiZ2P6F1MLU4vz4SZ4bOWumVr-fDK1vLDCJ5X6ZmoJxTXi27erkWOEkzAhNH5hIDwa97Dh2TieFzTCzRGeG0_Y9jt9cdS4VLOZpqk3XbXx-cKcsY2G9WI7HOb_t-hHLi-r_gUAAP__eW6Krg) who died when the commercial fishing vessel _Lily Jean_ sank without a mayday call off Gloucester, Massachusetts, launching a formal investigation into one of the deadliest fishing accidents in the state's recent history. The [72-foot vessel](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u2zAQhE9DdTQo6od2wSKNupc2pbCilta-UKTAXcvw7QPlCq-ZZjAfZubP1_wFlCg_f8OOXmKZM745oQjW-ajlG4Oc8EqyF5aKiWBJGGZr7KiN1aabV6D0mftm9RHRYtegb50zbTu68dFsfjFoh-ER7m649_cQII6LuzsL42qWh-ka8hfNWNO1rRmNuw1mGPq1c7FzEBCi6g2uJKXevgtl2TCmTzmx3kLZm-Q3kYNV90vZSdkpf47CclnKThdY2cm0yk7dJS_W1z5lJzk1C1QdiTesO2Qd4BCgrJ8v1gw5UqLjKDpUfOudmCk_dYlR78AMYXsxirAOBVg0RMGqlwJyRf8qOzXVr8ebUiIenerNcwdK_xvLj24-vf0XAAD__xtHk0Y) issued an automated distress beacon about 25 miles off Cape Ann on Friday, but searchers found only a debris field, one body, and an empty life raft. The Coast Guard has opened a formal investigation into the sinking. Those lost included Captain Accursio "Gus" Sanfilippo, a fifth-generation fisherman featured on History Channel's _Nor'Easter Men,_ beloved in [Gloucester's tight-knit fishing community](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0M1q3DAUBeCnkXcaZPlHzUKLlEaFhEkLCZRkY-5Y15471UhGumN33r64r9DdWRwOfOfXcTgCBYrzK1zR8pSGiFsJyIx5WHK64Mgr3AJfU-GMgeAUcBy00r1UWqpm8EDhPrSVtxOixqZCWxuj6ro3_UN1tnDqWtW0NSjfYtvViA81eg_Y6dMXY7qK7L6mtGrqWvXKHDrVda1vzNQYGBEm0Sr0xCkfLokin3EK97RiPozpWgV7Zl6KaB6FdkK7bdsOp1Q4Rd3tlL0ktNuj0C6kEYLQbg7pNmJhzHKicqY4S84wo7_L2-KBUVJcsTDNwJSipMhJllv8jVGuWAoGiX8WHBm95OThLrT78fH2_vLy2b89f3v6-fz6-PXTGfP9_en4IbSrsvXLRiFQ6Y1o1XwFCv8A_F-vr1b_DQAA___eTpmc). Mayor Paul Lundberg announced the victims' names would be added to a city memorial honoring thousands of fishermen lost at sea.
## List: Most Reliable Car Brands of 2026
Here's the [2026 list of the most reliable car brands](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL8GMiNjA54JHFxCNpdeiBq7PfScwcjuAc3fr1hpv2DzUlW99-8xPYAibc-_sKLlkKYNzxKRGfO05_RCxwe8I6-pcMZIMEd0k5JqEFIJqScPFD9TV3sbEBXqGm1rjGzbwQz3erGh06FFZ3DuAd0N_Gzc3esBMXTa32RN9mqTSuq2lYM0TS_7vvPaBG3AIYSqk-iJU25eiTZeMMRPOjA3Lq11tAvzXir9p1JjpcbzPJuDyhuig50YIhW-gpUaM2z_0QteUFw04gdHOMhizrD5ImgT15tKjXW2fj8pRiqDqTr5XIHi9yT_ytNh1VcAAAD___9wfrA) according to Consumer Reports, with Japanese automakers dominating the rankings by claiming six of the top seven spots. Toyota leads with a score of 66, followed closely by Subaru and Lexus, with all three manufacturers known for conservative engineering, long model cycles, and proven technology. Tesla recorded the biggest improvement in the rankings, climbing eight spots to ninth place thanks to stronger reliability scores for the Model 3 and Model Y after years of incremental design refinements. [See the full chart here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL8GMiNjA54JHFxCNpdeiBq7PfScwcjuAc3fr1hpv2DzUlW99-8xPYAibc-_sKLlkKYNzxKRGfO05_RCxwe8I6-pcMZIMEd0k5JqEFIJqScPFD9TV3sbEBXqGm1rjGzbwQz3erGh06FFZ3DuAd0N_Gzc3esBMXTa32RN9mqTSuq2lYM0TS_7vvPaBG3AIYSqk-iJU25eiTZeMMRPOjA3Lq11tAvzXir9p1JjpcbzPJuDyhuig50YIhW-gpUaM2z_0QteUFw04gdHOMhizrD5ImgT15tKjXW2fj8pRiqDqTr5XIHi9yT_ytNh1VcAAAD___9wfrA).

clean_body: President Trump to amass a $12 billion stockpile of critical and rare earth minerals, dubbed Project Vault, to slash U.S. reliance on Chinese materials and curb Beijing's leverage in trade talks. | | | | | | Tuesday, February 3, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1863, writer Samuel Clemens in stories for a Virginia City newspaper. He borrowed the name from his riverboating days, when the call meant the water was two fathoms deep. New Consumer Reports data show which car brands are expected to be the , with Japanese manufacturers leading the rankings, as we report below. A young woman has been awarded millions by a New York jury after suing over a double mastectomy she received as a minor during a gender transition. The verdict, the first of its kind to reach trial, raises questions about medical standards of care. Host Ayla Brown explains on today’s episode of The Flyover Podcast. . From today’s sponsor, : See why some investors say this patented coal-to-commodities breakthrough could be a rare “Rockefeller Moment” as the company works toward a potential NASDAQ listing. | Trump Launches $12B Critical Minerals Stockpile President Trump to amass a $12 billion stockpile of critical and rare-earth minerals, dubbed Project Vault, to slash U.S. reliance on Chinese materials and curb Beijing's leverage in trade talks. The stockpile will be from the U.S. Export-Import Bank and nearly $2 billion in private capital, with minerals procured and stored for automakers, the defense industry, tech firms, and other manufacturers. China currently controls between 60% and 70% of the rare-earth market, which provides foundational materials for modern technology ranging from batteries to MRI machines to F-35 fighter jets. The U.S. was entirely reliant on imports for and imported 50% or more of 29 others, according to the White House. Gloucester Boating Tragedy Claims Seven The U.S. Coast Guard on Monday who died when the commercial fishing vessel Lily Jean sank without a mayday call off Gloucester, Massachusetts, launching a formal investigation into one of the deadliest fishing accidents in the state's recent history. The issued an automated distress beacon about 25 miles off Cape Ann on Friday, but searchers found only a debris field, one body, and an empty life raft. The Coast Guard has opened a formal investigation into the sinking. Those lost included Captain Accursio "Gus" Sanfilippo, a fifth-generation fisherman featured on History Channel's Nor'Easter Men, beloved in . Mayor Paul Lundberg announced the victims' names would be added to a city memorial honoring thousands of fishermen lost at sea. List: Most Reliable Car Brands of 2026 Here's the according to Consumer Reports, with Japanese automakers dominating the rankings by claiming six of the top seven spots. Toyota leads with a score of 66, followed closely by Subaru and Lexus, with all three manufacturers known for conservative engineering, long model cycles, and proven technology. Tesla recorded the biggest improvement in the rankings, climbing eight spots to ninth place thanks to stronger reliability scores for the Model 3 and Model Y after years of incremental design refinements. . Houston-based Coterra Energy is [merging](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO2zAQRL-G6tYg14x0V6hwo-5SJqWw1K4sItTSIGnr_PeBnU9IN8BgHgbv99f8RTFFvf6kXcb2veZZ5ahJWpMy55h2KVcpIee20x8p4f6sR9QZLfZgEex5ZorpOfuOx2D503UyumGwDq2z2G2j87yG1dGw8MBOXLAr_hjY9_1nkI9-6OL4glm059fGu5Nf3fIh4gUDsyc23grHlsupbdLkm-qanvkh5bTkvUvj1tqtmvPF4GRwOo7jtFd9dQYnUbhXg9OeVZ4GpyXvN9Io9Z2blEJAysDyyAqkmu-6CBCg8xBiSjEr_HMAbRNgoQRLEWpSgUDlgC3fa8sKgaow5JjgGkmbwYkKXC7u16PEoysj344Xr_aD8fa6U0zv_-3_rD9G_BsAAP__nMmaog) with Oklahoma City's Devon Energy in a $21 billion all-stock deal to create a company with an enterprise value of $58 billion, making it one of the largest U.S. oil producers.

# Tuesday, February 3, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1956, former NFL wide receiver [John Jefferson](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4jAQRb_G6YjGgwmhcEGTAoltt7SczDgZ4djI8RL4-xXvF155i3uOdP7e3d1LlDT_8Svb-g7ZJd63yLVycVniymXmMuZcV__gMv77bLskh4DdAfAAR0de4seZhuwIdNENW30-g0bQgM1iYTrheNGG9AgeEHRPJsBp6klTRx02Yr8wQDh-P0a3JuipZzaMI5HxpAwwSc2lrQtXfvstxE9-cWmnvDbRLrU-N3W8KhwUDpzaXR7yZBLf5jIrHL5b4XDLS3I3DoHLlpNT2F9XLjL55ELOdfQxKrw0xdJzlxhl687KwLx6iT-m-rs-L4v_AwAA__9EDntV) was born in Dallas. After attending Arizona State University, Jefferson was drafted by the San Diego Chargers in 1978 with the 14th overall pick, becoming the first player to accumulate over 1,000 receiving yards in his first three years in the league. He also played for the Green Bay Packers and the Cleveland Browns. The University Interscholastic League unveiled new competition classifications for high school football, basketball, and volleyball for the next two years. Check out the details [here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFygzAQRL9GdDDSSTGhUJGGzmlTMkI60E0EYqSzsf8-Y_9C6p3dffN-rtPVUaJ9_XYbWn4sedrxrAmZsUyZ0oZlxTLnzJv7xTLfnvWkfQIJl1ZCK_UUHKXnZJpgZxkG1aBVfS8VSCWhiVah8sa7YZD6YswyLFrqRc9zGHoVQo8N2deYBKlfHaM6syj_iWgQ5hCMC8JIDMS5dByR8eHqkp75jqXzeWuSjcxHFfpLwChgPM-z87Hk_ZUKGOuRC1cBY6Q1Vh9zTgJGV5h8QgHjjVIbqHIhz21Bl2jdN9y5fT-1oDQMavjojng0xYbjpJSoXnph5Lo5Sm8I_p-6u4W_AAAA__9aJ4VT). Was your local high school affected? Let us know in today's _Poll_. A young woman has been awarded millions by a New York jury after suing over a double mastectomy she received as a minor during a gender transition. The verdict, the first of its kind to reach trial, raises questions about medical standards of care. Host Ayla Brown explains on today’s episode of _The Flyover Podcast_. [Tune in here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykzzFuwyAUgOHTwBbr8SAhGRi6ZEvXjuhhnmNUbCxMY_v2VXqFHuD7pf_r4R-UcpqfnzSxa_tQ_Mzbmrk1rr6kPHF9cg2ltIm-uYafY93S7BHwcgI8gfaRUj68kdEFiDcl2SlrQSEoQDm6c9C3cKXeGtRXNYQ-2EDRatIEvVJnmdw7Bgj6bYzqzKD6K7NhDDEaisIAx9RK7drIjXdah3yUF9euL5PMbmxtWYX-EHgXeK8cKs2xy4fA-1Ii762SktXFZUs5p_VihYHnRCn_-fa_65fD3wAAAP__2ZFtFw). From today’s sponsor, [FinanceBuzz](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAQhp8Gb7GOg5hkYKgUeUvXjgjMkaDgEGES229fuY3UB-h436e7-_V_nc3ZxhTvl087kq5LyOZO85SoViomxzRSuVBxOdfR3qi45zrN8W4QsNsB7kAYb2NajWy8duCPvCHNlQKOwAGbq5ZdNwT0R1JEB4S94tYRBqFISbEfRBP1dgwQxLYjeSsDHw5EktB5L61nEsjHmktbr1RpsVNIa35RaYc8Nklfa31MTHww7Bn28zy3a1lquW2aYW9DMAMTfQ6BiomeiRMil5xht6lfABzf8_R0yMSpJ_cHBBOn98-maP-YY0px6hSTcBltTD9B6v_qe2n8DgAA__9ScIMg)—learn how a 0% intro APR balance transfer card could help you stop paying interest and make real progress on credit card debt through 2027.

## Merger Creates Major U.S. Oil Producer
Houston-based Coterra Energy is [merging](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO2zAQRL-G6tYg14x0V6hwo-5SJqWw1K4sItTSIGnr_PeBnU9IN8BgHgbv99f8RTFFvf6kXcb2veZZ5ahJWpMy55h2KVcpIee20x8p4f6sR9QZLfZgEex5ZorpOfuOx2D503UyumGwDq2z2G2j87yG1dGw8MBOXLAr_hjY9_1nkI9-6OL4glm059fGu5Nf3fIh4gUDsyc23grHlsupbdLkm-qanvkh5bTkvUvj1tqtmvPF4GRwOo7jtFd9dQYnUbhXg9OeVZ4GpyXvN9Io9Z2blEJAysDyyAqkmu-6CBCg8xBiSjEr_HMAbRNgoQRLEWpSgUDlgC3fa8sKgaow5JjgGkmbwYkKXC7u16PEoysj344Xr_aD8fa6U0zv_-3_rD9G_BsAAP__nMmaog) with Oklahoma City's Devon Energy in a $21 billion all-stock deal to create a company with an enterprise value of $58 billion, making it one of the largest U.S. oil producers. The new entity will control roughly 750,000 acres in the Delaware portion of the Permian Basin, which is expected to account for more than half of its projected production of 1.6 million barrels of oil equivalent per day [by the third quarter](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLtutDAUhJ_GdEb2wVz-wsXql-g2ZaJUyJcDWDH2yjbL8vYRafIAKc_5Zkaj-bhPd-W8C8ub2lCW1xyngEf2WAqmKTq_YVow6RjLpr4w6f3MhwsTMOgoA8qaySrnz0lUVmpm__EKJe97xoFxBtUqwdh20DNo03RMt23LVGdbPszNAGLo28rJK4wBay6P4LWYuRkQBYK2VihLBEPrSkx1WbHgS-XZn_GJqTZxq7xcS3lk0twIjATG4zjqLYeLERgx0D0TGLNxGAxeH5XKSn9vi88YKAZMy0lLpHo_qYkFU1J0jokCpy3VznsXw8VNQlWQ5lV5pItToRAYVaK3G3_f_3_qKkn7OC597noi2LIp53-alr_t-5TwHQAA__9VfpEi). The company will retain Devon's name but be headquartered in Houston. Each Coterra share will be exchanged for 0.70 Devon shares, and a stock buyback of more than $5 billion is planned after the merger is finalized next quarter. The companies are [targeting savings](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OqzAQRp8GOqPxYEIoXNyGLrfdEo3xAKM1ODIOCW-_Yl9h--9H53w9hgdJkG3-Tyvb_JnisPF7D5wzpyFKWDnNnFyMeaVvTu517m_ZBgS8KUAF9eBJwjmY0lsHvtMlW922oBE0YLlYrKGZWj_q2vGIPDGP2iA1befMvQEqxV5jgFBfHaMrM-nxzmwYnfeGfGGAveSYqrxw5g_tUzjjwaka41oGu-T83Iv6X4F9gf0kG20jVyctMV6JAvsLqcDe8xE3RXNi3pV7nSpKUEkOCkrXiI3pbl215DWUyfrnW0KQ_dYWBuaVJPy-5b85Oiz-BAAA__9043xi) of $1 billion in operating costs by 2027. "_Scale of this magnitude unlocks operational and financial advantages that simply aren't available to operators of less scale_ ," Devon CEO [Clay Gaspar said](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO4zAMRL_G7mjQshL7ChXXuMu1VxqUyMTCylIgKXHy9wsH2C_YhgUH8zAz_y_LhXzw8faPNjH1dU1LlL0EqVXyknzYJN8k25TqRl-S7eNddh8XheoMqACHhcmH96JbNhb5T9-K6ccRe4U9qnY1k7aDI5xOg0OnT6O4AfUwTcxnJNuPrTcHDBUOh0f3nb72bhLRoiyzJm40CvuacldXqfKicg3v9JTcubS1way13ksz_G3U3Kh53_fOResOsVHzwW7UjOrnPAqUlYLAPSd-OMkFWJ4pAkUGl6rkTFATfIqDj0BwmsD6EHyKwEKhW-sW2mz4vh_fch4bjbeNfPgEqr-b8WnUdwAAAP__nGuHIg).
## Girl Injured in Road-Rage Shooting
A 10-year-old girl was hospitalized on Friday after a [road-rage shooting](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykULuynDAM_RrTaUcW7IOCIg3dTZuSEbYAzTX4jm2W5e8z5BfSqNCZ8_zzNXyxBt3m37xKVz5THDY5cpBSJA1RwyppljTGWFb-ljTuZz50GwjpAUiA9eBZwzk0le9G9K2tpLPPJ1pCi1QtnWtHft7xYe_U3mvXvl4vh_iQEQXrybaVdpcYEtYXp7G3ZrLuJdIIjd437E2D4rXEdCuLFPlwnsIZ35JuLq5V6JZSfrKpfxnqDfXHcdy-M5cLNNRfbQz1IToOhvrLylCP1lBfX4enST2_tcDKG7BzexYPcYK8xFh0m8EinMIJYvDg93S9UmQPiWeBSZN4mPcNDi0LeN7npUgC3eAti7oghvoqdf7n0BA0P56mwXllDf_Cl_-b_N3R3wAAAP__vUeWeg) in San Antonio. The suspect, 41-year-old Bryan Arceo, is in custody with bonds totaling $375,000. According to an affidavit, Arceo was driving his ex-wife to work and his daughter to school when he pulled out in front of a minivan driven by the victim's mother, prompting her to honk. Arceo reacted by "slamming on his brakes" and confronting the woman at an intersection, where he [pointed a gun](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUL1utDAQfBrTGa33_B0fhYs0dJc2JVrsBawYjGzf-Xj7iLxCupHmTzNfj_FBPvh9-aSNTXnPcdy55sClcBqjDxunhdMUY9nom9P0PHP1-4iAdwko4TY68uEcdePMBK5XDRvVdaAQFGCzGo09WbIaZmX_I7LFGa0ivndz_480NN5cYYBwuzxatfpSMmvGyTlNTmhg50tMbVm58JvyHM744tTauDXBrKUcWdw-BA4Ch1pre9Ia48UKHK45AgdKxdvAF0yRnEy0sMzPfLAtciUnY92l6hVohVq1a9lCk4w7qg_B53snNCwb-fDbWf721MvgTwAAAP__VIB-WQ) at her and fired a shot as she drove away. Following the incident, Arceo dropped off his ex-wife at work and took his 11-year-old daughter to school. His ex-wife, who was sitting in the back seat, said she looked away during the shooting but [confirmed](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkT2OpDAQRk9jsmqZsoefgGATstl0Q1TYBXjH4JZdPTS3XzFX2PjTe9LT9-dz-qQQw7H-pp0HeS9pOvgskUU4TynEnfPKeU5JdvriPL-ucoZjQo0NaARtJk8hXpOt_DBr39cVD3Xb6hp1rbHahs7wgtYYss4Yq7H3ru0adIuxzYchrMJwyzRqczO2ftildh2zZZy9t-SV1eyDpPyQjYXfVJZ4pW_OD5f2Kg6byLMo80vhqHA8z_PxxUf5uFeFI2UJLrLC8Q5TOMbkKCocCx1Ah6QjJPixQiF5wxpyhLIlgZzIQ6aVobzKk53AnK8byo4TUM5cBP7S8aJ8wd2gcMTWQMN93yzcwNI5BOtqDR3pHvp2tsZb0ztuqjz45xliDKVpldXrTiH-9Mj_vfA94L8AAAD__4J3m3g) Arceo is known to carry a silver and black handgun. Arceo is charged with three counts of aggravated assault and one count of endangering a child. Jason Gates said his daughter, Alisa, is "doing great" after [undergoing surgery](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ_GdBONB5TkFi62cXe33RIN8QBWbHxlT0J4-xX7Clsfnb_vz_f4zTHFbfnNWZx-5jJusrckqlLHElOWukidStHMT6nT62h73EZCugISYD8GjukYhy64CcOX7cTZ2w0toUXqVndFHq49y4z3LzsHO08ilqbbvZ9mS3fpojvDkLA_PYO9DLN93EUGoSmEgYMZUELUUi-6isqH25yO8pZ6eZTcJbeq_jTT_zLkDfl93y_PxnqKhvz5xpBP5cHJkD-rDHm0hnyPhvwqB4QY4CgvWEShrUWhMWTeoEqKb2mQS5ZNgbelHhBqfEsFLeUJHDOwgkU4hCuUFCDwa1lVqiHfVRd-9phSbNebGXDJHNO_zfp_pN-O_gYAAP__iXaT2Q).
## Space Shuttle Columbia Disaster Remembered
Sunday marked 23 years since the Space Shuttle Columbia [disintegrated during reentry](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD1ygzAQRk8jOphlUcAuVKShc9qUjIRWsBMJeSTZsm-fIVdI__3Me9-35abZ87F96UCqvFxcDqrZUymUlsg-UNoomRhL0D-UzOOdKx8LAo4tYAvDYjX79yIbqwzYa9-Q6qcJeoQesNmVHFfQcnDGXEYy48foAPV1MhKMdHY1DatzDBCGsyP7Trp-vRBJQmOt1FZIIMslpq7sVOils_Pv-KTUrTE0Xu2l3LMYPgXOAudaa-f5SUesLr7OiMD5ZBI4JwoUDCU-tjbf9Upt3h-leGrX6B_BsG6SsvfK3nMeJyFhC5r930_5n52nwt8AAAD__8c0fOk) over East Texas. All seven astronauts on board died in the explosion, and the disaster is etched into the memories of locals who witnessed it and participated in the massive search and recovery effort. Over 25,000 volunteers [searched more than 2.3 million acres](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOozAQBuCnMZ3RMPYlULi4hi7XXonG9pBYMTiyHQhvv2KlfYJtphn93y_9_2_TjUIM6_0fLWzqZ07TynuJXCvnKYW4cL5ztinVhZ6c7fsoe1gnBLxIQAlq8hTiMenGGwt-6Bo23fUKHUIH2DxM7-YenLIw2IGc0npwf6zt-vmivLNKNcGcGCCoM6O7Vs-d65k1o_Vekxca2IeaclsfXPlDZY5H2ji3Li1NNI9aX0WovwJHgeO-7-0z1u18ChxPW-AI-HOYSpWnsha5UH5KVNlLWtewcS6UD-lSfC82kPShUKmcBY5NNv61hxhDuVyFhvtCIX7319-tthn8CgAA__-lzIH0) for debris and the remains of the crew. The Remembering Columbia Museum was established in Hemphill in 2011. Members of a local Civil Air Patrol [visited the museum](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQhZ_GdI7GgzfcFC62obvbbon8Mwmj2BjZBsLbr9hXuM1pjs6no-_v9_RtOfLy-mMTmfZ55mmho0ZqjcqUOSYqLyou55bsm4rbznrwMiHgXQJK6KdgOZ6T7oJxEB6qI6OGARSCAuxmox5PPcBjcHcHv5xy9vFlAd3dD7r3HlTH5oIBQn9ttLrpp_JfRJrQhaBtEBoocMvl1mZq9LH1Gc-8U7n5nLpo5tbWKvrfAkeB43Ect3ds-1UKHC-2wBHwCiVw9LxzlJaLXG0rOcqdK7cqfY5bcmxloUTJFbt4kmmrtCVJn5kdNzlTWmeOUWIv7bLwTqXacgocu2LCenCMXO-D0PBKluP_f-1nVneD_wIAAP__ayONzw) on Saturday, and a remembrance ceremony was held on Sunday. Beech Grove Volunteer Fire Department Chief Jamie Gunter [said](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuO8yAUhJ8Gd0RwzI_jguJv3GXbLS0uB_soECIgt7dfeV9h69HM6Pu-L-vFUqLb9mUzmv6OZb3hqyXsHetaKGWsG1ZXSs_2itU9Pu1FtxUEaC6Ai3ENltJnVUMwToRZDmjkNAkJQgoYduOlnoNyGv7Nyk8wRXRKz0r7OJ_1qM8DmWNMgBiPjpInFaU_IyoEF4KygSmBgXqpp75jx7dtMX3KE-vJlzwks_d-b2z8z2BhsFxjyEfAYDlIGCypeJsYLA7R73yr5Yk8UkXud8LIK2bMDmvjvqRHdmS5r7btQzXh_qKUqOmJKbFlS-n3sv9N1NPATwAAAP__mq5-0g) he was "very proud of the job that everyone did" during the search and recovery. Dr. Scott Lieberman, who captured photos of the disaster, called it "a devastating moment." "_The event had a profound effect on me, not only because of the tragedy itself but also due to the realization of the impact such images can have_ ," [Lieberman reflected](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGuszAQBODTmA60LASHwsXf0OVvX4mMdwErBkf2JoTbPyVXePVovpHm5zberA9-X_7bjY285zjufOTAIpzG6MPGaeE0xSibvXOanmc-_D4iYFcCltCMZH04x7YgMwH1dcGm1hpqhBqwWI2jDpymTrsG-rmfdd9c0V2b6aLnCzEV3nwwQGg-nbau2rl2V-aWcSJqLakWmLzEVMnKwm-b53DGF6fKxa0IZhV5ZNX8UzgoHI7jqO4s90-ocMjO8-5Y4eBieG6Tt2VenyKBS0l2YTpLtlnKr6twKJKhx-FD8LnTqoVlsz58h-Rv97wM_gYAAP__ntd8Gw). "_It was a heartbreaking moment, as these were my childhood heroes_."

Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Merger Creates Major U.S. Oil Producer Houston-based Coterra Energy is with Oklahoma City's Devon Energy in a $21 billion all-stock deal to create a company with an enterprise value of $58 billion, making it one of the largest U.S. oil producers. The new entity will control roughly 750,000 acres in the Delaware portion of the Permian Basin, which is expected to account for more than half of its projected production of 1.6 million barrels of oil equivalent per day . The company will retain Devon's name but be headquartered in Houston. Each Coterra share will be exchanged for 0.70 Devon shares, and a stock buyback of more than $5 billion is planned after the merger is finalized next quarter. The companies are of $1 billion in operating costs by 2027. "Scale of this magnitude unlocks operational and financial advantages that simply aren't available to operators of less scale ," Devon CEO . Girl Injured in Road-Rage Shooting A 10-year-old girl was hospitalized on Friday after a in San Antonio. The suspect, 41-year-old Bryan Arceo, is in custody with bonds totaling $375,000. According to an affidavit, Arceo was driving his ex-wife to work and his daughter to school when he pulled out in front of a minivan driven by the victim's mother, prompting her to honk. Arceo reacted by "slamming on his brakes" and confronting the woman at an intersection, where he at her and fired a shot as she drove away. Following the incident, Arceo dropped off his ex-wife at work and took his 11-year-old daughter to school. His ex-wife, who was sitting in the back seat, said she looked away during the shooting but Arceo is known to carry a silver and black handgun. Arceo is charged with three counts of aggravated assault and one count of endangering a child. Jason Gates said his daughter, Alisa, is "doing great" after . Space Shuttle Columbia Disaster Remembered Sunday marked 23 years since the Space Shuttle Columbia over East Texas. All seven astronauts on board died in the explosion, and the disaster is etched into the memories of locals who witnessed it and participated in the massive search and recovery effort. Over 25,000 volunteers for debris and the remains of the crew. The Remembering Columbia Museum was established in Hemphill in 2011. Members of a local Civil Air Patrol on Saturday, and a remembrance ceremony was held on Sunday. Beech Grove Volunteer Fire Department Chief Jamie Gunter he was "very proud of the job that everyone did" during the search and recovery. Dr. Scott Lieberman, who captured photos of the disaster, called it "a devastating moment." "The event had a profound effect on me, not only because of the tragedy itself but also due to the realization of the impact such images can have ," . subject: Portland City Council and Mayor Demands Governor Mills Hit the Brakes: Unanimous Vote Urges 60-Day Eviction Freeze as ICE Fear Ripples Through City
body:

PORTLAND, Maine -The Portland City Council and Mayor Mark Dion voted unanimously Monday night to send a letter to Janet Mills (D) urging her to declare a state of emergency and impose a 60-day moratorium on evictions, citing what speakers described as mounting fear and instability tied to increased U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) [...]

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: The US government shutdown is shaking Washington to its core! A small mistake by President Trump, has set off a downward spiral that threatens the entire US economy. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | The US government shutdown is shaking Washington to its core! Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

| | [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001g2AJUr5yDjhjaowc6GmX_JxvV0IoiKvfvDaqXCy9Nyal1TFT7Za3StqaCZponQwLxQWipxAE84NxGw6Ls5QcZGego_bRKPaNh1uRnVGeYsPM2PEIgP2jLpUPYNSXzwaLK01-6itabGKcmzWePS1nOmAC554BrRxfmF0s_47ICFE6mABLAfN3fmCYvCqX6brSsByUL2zyGIoEAKb0Y5XInYXq_T2ys9PBgS3y2aS1Txc=&c=cJVJwm74nSj3A_EWGgc3UEpgvDUQaVp3LhwahqrgJ_DebiGPLLHzqA==&ch=yCViy44c1trIWUkyqJsO-1GlrJGmcAhay0JS745RFjE0R7eADu-ICg==)
---
| Peter,
Both of my parents were raised on farms in Tensas Parish, Louisiana. My father by a sharecropper and my mother on a plantation.
They picked cotton by hand. For them it was survival and my grandparents did not believe that a formal education was necessary. For them missing school to go to work on the farm was the norm.
The only time my Dad could go to school was when the weather was so bad that nothing could be done on the farm. Kids would tease him saying, "Must be raining cause Stump (his nickname) is here and not at the farm."
The highest level of education my dad reached was the 8th grade but despite this, his informal education taught him to run a successful farm and he became the first in our family to own land.
---
| [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001g2AJUr5yDjhjaowc6GmX_JxvV0IoiKvfvDaqXCy9Nyal1TFT7Za3StqaCZponQwLxQWipxAE84NxGw6Ls5QcZGego_bRKPaNh1uRnVGeYsPM2PEIgP2jLpUPYNSXzwaLK01-6itabGKcmzWePS1nOmAC554BrRxfmF0s_47ICFE6mABLAfN3fmCYvCqX6brSsByUL2zyGIoEAKb0Y5XInYXq_T2ys9PBVWtGO3GZ3C0=&c=cJVJwm74nSj3A_EWGgc3UEpgvDUQaVp3LhwahqrgJ_DebiGPLLHzqA==&ch=yCViy44c1trIWUkyqJsO-1GlrJGmcAhay0JS745RFjE0R7eADu-ICg==)
---
One of the last surviving photos of my father. Taken at my high school graduation in 1988. Pardon the graininess.
---
My Dad gave me the opportunity to chart a different course and I was the first person in my family to graduate from high school and I went on to attend LSU.
Ultimately, Baton Rouge was too big a city with too much temptation for the farm boy from the country town of Waterproof and I returned home with my tail between my legs, sent back on academic probation with my first child on the way.
My father asked me sarcastically, "What are you going to do now Mr. Man?" And I told him earnestly, "I'm going to farm just like you do."
And that's what I've been doing to this day. Starting off with the 220 acres that my Dad leased, we worked to grow the farm to where we stand today -- farming 3,200 acres, 700 of which belong to my family.
---
| [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001g2AJUr5yDjhjaowc6GmX_JxvV0IoiKvfvDaqXCy9Nyal1TFT7Za3StqaCZponQwLxQWipxAE84NxGw6Ls5QcZGego_bRKPaNh1uRnVGeYsPM2PEIgP2jLpUPYNSXzwaLK01-6itabGKcmzWePS1nOmAC554BrRxfmF0s_47ICFE6mABLAfN3fmCYvCqX6brSsByUL2zyGIoEAKb0Y5XInYXq_T2ys9PBHrpf-9U-TEc=&c=cJVJwm74nSj3A_EWGgc3UEpgvDUQaVp3LhwahqrgJ_DebiGPLLHzqA==&ch=yCViy44c1trIWUkyqJsO-1GlrJGmcAhay0JS745RFjE0R7eADu-ICg==)
---
My son and I on the farm in 2021.
---
Unfortunately, in my 55 years of life here in Louisiana much has changed for the worse. Life has been sucked out of our small towns by monopolies like Dollar General and Walmart. Agriculture has become far less profitable, dominated by big business, and I have watched all of my Dad's friends that were black farmers go out of business.
I'm too old to learn a new trick. I've got 13 grandkids. I've watched my Dad break his body down through manual labor on this farm and saw my son lose part of his leg in a farming accident here. We've put too much of our blood into this soil. We can't just pick up this farm and move.
Our beloved home is turning into something unrecognizable. Louisianans on both sides of the aisle recognize this and they are ready for radical change.
I have to take a stand and fight for the way of life that we had when I grew up here as a boy and it's why I am proud to be the Democrat running for Senate in Louisiana.
Dems can win here statewide -- look no further than our last governor Democrat John Bel Edwards. And now a blue wave is forming, putting Louisiana in play for Democrats like me who can articulate an authentic vision of FDR style economic populism that can compete with and defeat the fake populism of MAGA.
Taking this seat means electing Louisiana's first black senator and statewide elected official since reconstruction. It means we reclaim the Senate with an unshakeable majority -- the Trump administration goes down in flames. [Winning Louisiana is now possible and it's why I'm asking you to split a donation between me and Trisha Calvarese of $25, $10, or even just $5 to help Democrats flip the Senate and take back the House in 2026. ](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001g2AJUr5yDjhjaowc6GmX_JxvV0IoiKvfvDaqXCy9Nyal1TFT7Za3StqaCZponQwL2mE_T5X68C5boFiCQXMdjiFtXOAo6bn-nKPHnXkDP8CPc5FuiB4zUtFMSsfJoTWvHkWdugPyQGSi0HBPK1sSIEcMMP_dEEm123B790S-FSsNKMM4jew1ZVbfIBsPOmHi-F0Wz7_PsiRcB9TD2rn3OC6cD8r_FnKnJYTDxgrsVhoZvLDsrEk7lw==&c=cJVJwm74nSj3A_EWGgc3UEpgvDUQaVp3LhwahqrgJ_DebiGPLLHzqA==&ch=yCViy44c1trIWUkyqJsO-1GlrJGmcAhay0JS745RFjE0R7eADu-ICg==)
