name: Movement 4 Freedom
subject: We’re proud to endorse Jocelyn Sida for Mayor of Fontana
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/e559868487e7/tockcw1cwy-257?e=81ab29c4d2)
---
[|
---](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=faef818619&e=81ab29c4d2)
| | | | | | Peter,
---
| | Movement for Freedom is proud to officially endorse Jocelyn Sida for Mayor of Fontana, California.
Joz isn’t a career politician — **she’s a politivist, organizer, and lifelong Fontana resident** who has spent her career fighting for environmental justice and putting communities of color first.
---
[|
---](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=81fd2c0ec0&e=81ab29c4d2)
| | At a time when too many leaders protect corporate interests and entrenched power, **Joz is focused on what actually matters:** safe neighborhoods, affordable living, clean air and water, and dignity for every family in Fontana.
Her work has always centered the people most impacted by injustice — and as mayor, she will bring that same courage, empathy, and vision to City Hall.
We need leaders who aren’t afraid to challenge wealth and power.Leaders who govern with humanity.Leaders who fight for communities — not donors.
Joz is that leader.
As she shared:
---
| | _“I’m grateful for the support of Movement for Freedom and for their shared commitment to dignity, safety, and justice for all. This campaign is rooted in a simple belief: that families in Fontana deserve to feel safe, that our children deserve to grow up healthy and free from pollution and harm, and that our community deserves a quality of life we can be proud of. Standing together with Movement for Freedom and the people of Fontana, I know we can build a Fontana where families can put down roots and thrive.”_
---
| [JOIN THE MOVEMENT](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=8b195c6eba&e=81ab29c4d2)
---
| | Endorsements are more than words — they’re a commitment to show up.
When you support the Movement for Freedom, you help us uplift leaders like Joz, protect progressive victories, and build real power in communities across the country.
👉 [Chip in today to help elect politivists who put people first.](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=945bfe5895&e=81ab29c4d2)
---
| |
_**The Movement for Freedom Team**_

Her work has always centered the people most impacted by injustice — and as mayor, she will bring that same courage, empathy, and vision to City Hall. We need leaders who aren’t afraid to challenge wealth and power.Leaders who govern with humanity.Leaders who fight for communities — not donors. Joz is that leader. As she shared: | | “I’m grateful for the support of Movement for Freedom and for their shared commitment to dignity, safety, and justice for all. This campaign is rooted in a simple belief: that families in Fontana deserve to feel safe, that our children deserve to grow up healthy and free from pollution and harm, and that our community deserves a quality of life we can be proud of. Standing together with Movement for Freedom and the people of Fontana, I know we can build a Fontana where families can put down roots and thrive.” | | | Endorsements are more than words — they’re a commitment to show up. When you support the Movement for Freedom, you help us uplift leaders like Joz, protect progressive victories, and build real power in communities across the country. 👉 | | The Movement for Freedom Team | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2026 Movement 4 Freedom. All rights reserved.

# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124660909/599297079/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZmMzNmM4Yi0zZDAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N81ioHwOBUsXevf6DopnHv7aNJxIdf1Y4Pt53hwzZOA=)

Donald Trump has been obsessed with shutting down scrutiny of the Epstein case. Documents mentioning him have appeared, disappeared, and reappeared. Survivors’ names were left exposed. Meanwhile, the DOJ insists this is about “protecting victims.”
That’s simply not true.
We deserve answers. Survivors deserve accountability. And the law demands full compliance.
**That’s why I’m calling on the Department of Justice to immediately release all remaining Epstein files, submit its legally required report to Congress, and stop playing games with the truth.**
**[Please, sign your name on my petition right now to join the call.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124660912/599297082/1627786981?refcode=260203_RG_doj-admits-pet&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZmMzNmM4Yi0zZDAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N81ioHwOBUsXevf6DopnHv7aNJxIdf1Y4Pt53hwzZOA=&emci=49a56f85-3d01-f111-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&emdi=dfc36c8b-3d01-f111-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&ceid=16877361)**

Robert Garcia

body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240663%2E444211%2EdeTqh-)
Did you see this amazing news?
**Democrat Taylor Rehmet just won a Texas state senate race in a district Trump won by 17 points.**
That’s a 31-point swing in just 15 months. A massive political shockwave.
People are rejecting Trump and MAGA candidates. After more than a year of flagrant corruption, cruelty, healthcare cuts, and high prices, people are showing up in droves to vote for change. Tarrant County, where Rehmet won, is known as one of the last reliably red urban counties.
_But our hard work is changing that._
By running candidates like Taylor who are fighting for the people, we can beat MAGA anywhere.
Taylor was outspent $6 MILLION to just over $380k. That should be an unwinnable race. But it proves what we already know: **people power beats big money every time.**
Now, Democrats are on offense. We are marshaling people power across the state to expand the map and pull off more stunning upsets this November.
Powered by People is fueling voter turnout across the state to make that happen. In red and blue counties, we’re hiring organizers and registering voters. We’re looking ahead to November, and we need you on our team.
[**Can you make a $3 donation to Powered by People to fund our work through the general election? We’re ready for more stunning upsets, but those are only possible with your support.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260203.-Tj6GC&refcode=ema20260203-240663&t=3&akid=240663%2E444211%2EdeTqh-)

name: Sharice Davids
subject: Tour pics attached!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
Take a look at some of the pictures!

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661102/599298028/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjA0ODc4ZC0zZTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgl2H9tq3xjSDLAOcAldfri2RSpMWg8rzSMyzDnZzmk=) Wow. The last week has been a whirlwind. I just wrapped up my statewide Kansas Strong Tour, and let me tell you: There is no state more passionate, more hard-working, and yes, more independent than ours. I heard it again and again on this tour: **Kansans aren’t asking for much.** Folks just want an affordable place to live, real opportunities to get ahead, and a government that has _their_ backs — not the wealthy and well-connected. **_[I’ll run through the highlights of my Kansas Strong Tour in a second. But before I go on, I need you to know: Because of folks like you, we’ve built a movement that extends far beyond the borders of KS-03. Now, I need your help to keep it going strong. Please, will you chip in $15 now to help make sure working families all across Kansas continue to have an ally in Congress?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661103/599298029/454992728?amounts=5,15,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=15&refcode=email_020326_tourpics&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjA0ODc4ZC0zZTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgl2H9tq3xjSDLAOcAldfri2RSpMWg8rzSMyzDnZzmk=)_** Okay, I know I promised pictures — here are the highlights! [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661104/599298030/454992728?amounts=5,15,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=15&refcode=email_020326_tourpics&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjA0ODc4ZC0zZTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgl2H9tq3xjSDLAOcAldfri2RSpMWg8rzSMyzDnZzmk=) I kicked off the tour in Topeka, speaking with labor leaders over a good breakfast. These days, a lot of politicians love to offer lip service to working people here in Kansas... but nothing more. **Not me.** Fair wages, decent benefits, safe workplaces, you name it — I’m fighting for it in Congress. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661105/599298031/454992728?amounts=5,15,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=15&refcode=email_020326_tourpics&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjA0ODc4ZC0zZTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgl2H9tq3xjSDLAOcAldfri2RSpMWg8rzSMyzDnZzmk=) We also had time to make a quick stop at the Kansas State Capitol to celebrate Kansas Day... where one quote at the Visitor’s Center particularly stood out to me! [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661106/599298032/454992728?amounts=5,15,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=15&refcode=email_020326_tourpics&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjA0ODc4ZC0zZTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgl2H9tq3xjSDLAOcAldfri2RSpMWg8rzSMyzDnZzmk=) The next day, we drove over to Wichita, where I saw Kansas’ workforce talent firsthand at Wichita State University’s Aviation Center. Kansas has all the talent, the infrastructure, and the know-how to bring so many good-paying jobs here. We just need more leaders with the determination to get it done. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661107/599298033/454992728?amounts=5,15,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=15&refcode=email_020326_tourpics&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjA0ODc4ZC0zZTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgl2H9tq3xjSDLAOcAldfri2RSpMWg8rzSMyzDnZzmk=) Finally, after chatting with small business owners in Dodge City, we wrapped up the tour in Colby at the Frahm Family Farm, where we discussed all the challenges farmers and ranchers are facing right now under an economy that’s going great for a select few... but leaving working folks behind. Kansas is a strong, resilient state. We have all the potential in the world... as long as we elect leaders who are willing to put in the work. I’ve learned so much over the last week — and I’m ready to take those lessons straight to Washington. But if we want to make sure national politicians actually _hear_ those lessons? **That’s where you come in.** **_[Please, will you chip in whatever you can to power our movement here in Kansas? Over the next nine months, we have to build the strongest team possible before Election Day — and we can only do it with small-dollar donations from folks like you.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661108/599298034/454992728?amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&refcode=email_020326_tourpics&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjA0ODc4ZC0zZTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgl2H9tq3xjSDLAOcAldfri2RSpMWg8rzSMyzDnZzmk=)_** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661109/599298035/454992728?express_lane=true&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&amount=5&refcode=email_020326_tourpics&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjA0ODc4ZC0zZTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgl2H9tq3xjSDLAOcAldfri2RSpMWg8rzSMyzDnZzmk=)

clean_body: Take a look at some of the pictures! = Wow. The last week has been a whirlwind. I just wrapped up my statewide Kansas Strong Tour, and let me tell you: There is no state more passionate, more hard-working, and yes, more independent than ours. I heard it again and again on this tour: Kansans aren’t asking for much. Folks just want an affordable place to live, real opportunities to get ahead, and a government that has their backs — not the wealthy and well-connected. = Okay, I know I promised pictures — here are the highlights! = I kicked off the tour in Topeka, speaking with labor leaders over a good breakfast. These days, a lot of politicians love to offer lip service to working people here in Kansas... but nothing more. Not me. Fair wages, decent benefits, safe workplaces, you name it — I’m fighting for it in Congress. = We also had time to make a quick stop at the Kansas State Capitol to celebrate Kansas Day... where one quote at the Visitor’s Center particularly stood out to me! = The next day, we drove over to Wichita, where I saw Kansas’ workforce talent firsthand at Wichita State University’s Aviation Center. Kansas has all the talent, the infrastructure, and the know-how to bring so many good-paying jobs here. We just need more leaders with the determination to get it done. = Finally, after chatting with small business owners in Dodge City, we wrapped up the tour in Colby at the Frahm Family Farm, where we discussed all the challenges farmers and ranchers are facing right now under an economy that’s going great for a select few... but leaving working folks behind. Kansas is a strong, resilient state. We have all the potential in the world... as long as we elect leaders who are willing to put in the work. I’ve learned so much over the last week — and I’m ready to take those lessons straight to Washington. But if we want to make sure national politicians actually hear those lessons? That’s where you come in. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = With gratitude, Sharice Davids | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. body:

We're honored to have you on our team, **Peter.**

subject: It wasn’t an easy decision to make
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661866/599301406/-1397648958?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_TAN_VIP_AC_20260203_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDYzYjAwYTMtNDEwMS1mMTExLTgzMmYtMDAwZDNhMWYwZTRjIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=qnw9arKfI71jYUbk5uvQrMN6CIKCQgSzZtgHBxtUwts=)
---
Team, it’s Katie Hobbs. I want to tell you about an important decision I made a few years ago. **If you’re short on time and want to skip ahead to split a donation between me and AZ Dems, you can use this link: ****[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/azd-em-hobbs26](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661867/599301407/836867298?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_TAN_VIP_AC_20260203_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDYzYjAwYTMtNDEwMS1mMTExLTgzMmYtMDAwZDNhMWYwZTRjIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=qnw9arKfI71jYUbk5uvQrMN6CIKCQgSzZtgHBxtUwts=)** _Otherwise, keep reading:_ During the 2020 election, I was Arizona’s Secretary of State. I had a job to do: **Count every vote.** Donald Trump and the extremists surrounding him didn’t make it easy. Like millions of Americans, I watched as an enraged crowd breached the halls of our nation’s Capitol in Washington, D.C. to overturn the people’s vote. **When the “Stop the Steal” movement took hold, I was harassed and even received death threats because of my role in overseeing Arizona’s elections.** Some would believe that in moments like these, you only have two options — fight, or give in. **I chose to get the job done**. We counted every vote, and in the middle of a once-in-a-century pandemic, we proved that democracy works. But I knew the work wasn’t over. It was clear that the MAGA right wouldn’t offer any real solutions; they’d just offer more and more conspiracy theories and scapegoats. So I decided to launch my campaign for governor to deliver transparency, accountability, and results for Arizonans. My opponents and national Republicans spent tens of millions of dollars and lobbed baseless attacks to try and defeat me, but thanks to hundreds of thousands of grassroots supporters chipping in what they could, we won with less than 1% of the vote. That brings us to _today._ Four years later, we’ve delivered incredible progress for Arizonans — lowering healthcare costs, investing in public education, and cutting taxes for middle-class families, just to name a few things. But there’s still more work to do. I’m running to deliver another four years of progress like this, but Donald Trump, my three GOP opponents, and the national Republican establishment are determined to unseat me and flip this seat **red.** Experts are calling this year’s Arizona governor’s race a **pure toss-up** , just like they did in 2022. But I’m telling you now — this race is going to be even more competitive and even more expensive than the first time. I know it might feel early to be reading emails like this, but these are the stakes. This is the level of competition in this race. And if we’re going to win this, we have to build a serious grassroots campaign from the very start. [**So please, split $10, $25, or whatever amount you’re able to my campaign and AZ Dems to help me win this 50/50 race, defend our hard-won progress, and serve another four years as governor of Arizona. I can’t do this without you!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661868/599301408/836867298?amount=10&refcode=AU_AZD_FR_TAN_VIP_AC_20260203_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDYzYjAwYTMtNDEwMS1mMTExLTgzMmYtMDAwZDNhMWYwZTRjIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=qnw9arKfI71jYUbk5uvQrMN6CIKCQgSzZtgHBxtUwts=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to the Arizona Democratic Party and Katie Hobbs will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124661869/599301409/836867298?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_AZD_FR_TAN_VIP_AC_20260203_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDYzYjAwYTMtNDEwMS1mMTExLTgzMmYtMDAwZDNhMWYwZTRjIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=qnw9arKfI71jYUbk5uvQrMN6CIKCQgSzZtgHBxtUwts=)

name: Demand Justice PAC
subject: It’s absurd and embarrassing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260203-61142&t=1001&akid=61142%2E1407376%2EQhl-sY ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260203-61142&t=1002&akid=61142%2E1407376%2EQhl-sY ]Demand Justice PAC

Tomorrow’s
judicial hearings should be simple.

Donald Trump keeps nominating cronies to lifetime seats on the bench —
loyalists who refuse to acknowledge that Joe Biden won in 2020 or condemn
January 6, and who, out of fear of offending him, put his power above the
law.

And somehow, they keep moving forward.

Why? Because 18 Senate Democrats keep helping advance them.

Yes... those Senate Democrats. The ones who love to give floor speeches
about democracy on the brink. The ones who warn about Trump’s
authoritarian tactics. The ones who preach about defending the courts, and
then quietly help Trump’s loyalists inch closer to lifetime appointments.

It’s absurd. It’s embarrassing. And it’s blatant hypocrisy.

You don’t get credit for opposing Trump’s court takeover while greasing
the wheels for it. You don’t get to wring your hands about the rule of law
while confirming judges who would happily twist it to serve Trump.

These Senate Democrats love to talk the talk.
Now it’s time to walk the walk.

Tomorrow’s hearing is the moment. This is where Senate Democrats stop
playing both sides, stop hiding behind process, and start doing the right
thing — by voting NO on any nominee whose allegiance is to Trump, not the
people.

We’re putting on the pressure, calling out the hypocrisy, and making it
impossible to ignore what’s at stake. But we can’t do it alone.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260203.JGkg_v&refcode=em20260203-61142&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61142%2E1407376%2EQhl-sY ]Chip in $5 ahead of tomorrow’s hearings to Demand
Justice PAC to help us turn up the heat and force these Senate Democrats
to stop advancing Trump’s cronies and finally vote NO.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260203.JGkg_v&amount=5&refcode=em20260203-61142&t=1022&akid=61142%2E1407376%2EQhl-sY ]Donate $5 ››

Thank you,

Demand Justice PAC

name: Team Katie
subject: You need to see the campaign finance reports
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
A Republican is the top fundraiser in the race for California governor.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40795%2E3422442%2EMlYdBK)
Peter,
Campaign finance reports are out and this has us worried. Take a look. 👇
[](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260203_1_ak.kvZo1J&refcode=em20260203-40795&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40795%2E3422442%2EMlYdBK)
**A Republican is the top fundraiser in the race for California governor.**
We’ve warned that Republicans are gaining momentum in California. First, _two Republicans led in the polls._ Now, **one of them raised more money than anyone else in the second half of 2025.**
A fundraising advantage matters in a state as large as California. It means you can run more ads, travel to every corner of the state, and get your message out to more voters.
Katie Porter can win this race, but only with grassroots support. If everyone reading this email chipped in $5, we could use that surge of support to put Katie in a winning position.
**[So we’re asking you directly: can you chip in $5 today? With your support, we can win over undecided voters and build the momentum we need to win the primary election.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260203_1_ak.kvZo1J&refcode=em20260203-40795&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40795%2E3422442%2EMlYdBK)**

**I never really considered running for office. Ever. I was too focused on raising my family, and being the best football sideline reporter I could be.**
**But even in the last few weeks, Minnesota has become the embodiment of everything wrong with politics today. Tim Walz, his inner circle, and the corrupt political class are too busy:**
**
* Wasting billions of our tax dollars on fraudulent daycares
* Failing to condemn the activists storming churches
* Raising prices on hardworking families
* Sabotaging anything the President wants to do
* Forcing men into women's spaces
* Pitting Americans against each other

**
**I can 't sit on the sidelines anymore. That's why I'm throwing my hat in the ring and announcing my candidacy for U.S. Senate!
[Is there ANYTHING I can say to convince you to become one of my first donors? The far is already racing to destroy my campaign before it even gets off the ground. I can’t shock the political world and flip this seat without your help.](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/pSzmNthb9SP-ijoFDUmRMn5lROuzhnvqhVJBlowaKratO12LQCGlilWkq4gzTD1mHCJQ6ZV3Q6hFnRxtkpph3LfGXZy_mQ47zSrzmCS-YQ4p7rWNltNZ0KpaKw4EicTZuk6banABEDkGaz5YY5tyO1c_1bZqrkgPGr9ELK0TMiPHjY14-pPhpVhuT2fysyg_LPlaAT-8qP2dLWDVg5iKzBPXWxRRyuJV4jpMvZgPe4Nsa2fH0dxrRRX0-2TORDbT7FA_QykaNNyEBx4sktvK8Arz4TQxB6bzPaegWHzQqeJozBHCEhx6wlS5gR401UD8-lMp4LF9pvSjgcA4cSx-b4dnsGSmSV0lvX045WjLuABAP4OI0h_gxYMoLdeDZ2mekIPfAt-FZ6t0gwxdAFb2UazBm5XgxFqCE2-GaE-UEJsAmoriZBY3MvB5huUWjEA89pNCUD56vpIY7tjgLm8p2lqruVbfue02j0umqNaWVymrATt7Uj7I4GHKZZQQWEfIHODTW9h1Id5lhL3nxuckXf2TX0tTM01vLiwx6sP-hh0bBEcbixfdIwIV6K9mIxF8kwS3G1KRHilKcfSy9lSBHSdcZmS8hPzbN6uACxVGKSk42oYucG0lCcUsLqb41nWUg6--fzO31_9ZGB7GGtTWzKpbMbLmtcesGq7OkQ8QbaTsRau0_9--wHu9w2j0aWoDy9AtGIFRg0RUGcqK86RVVK7JfMB9EMgrAxRGTwJ07nfj2OR8CmMaXBx-Y-86wu0nhZ8v9rq1TrfigS-oAwtrg1-JBUmi3HOUFZGdeSoTM-z9gLp7pmfv0lx2Ti8Rwgt1EQ/-Qkx3_0eAc38r7DrlafMem-sKCXRmrR3/17)[BECOME A FOUNDING DONOR
](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/4SM5_9A79GG8P8b281dsr8Fe5ME90MNhbZegGkmeMifzxKGCuOm-tAnRutSSjP_omNEAAxAFBGOa7YQm8qXDqLBMRxxkYjGqUcVBMp-t9Xxgj1drLaQtSdul2VP2pUN7TZRSl58tLGipL0iZ0e6bzcafO3q-szdjY7GZA5hjo3aaSWE6A8n-gEjqufjcnecxUlj-0ud1RrPCigKNz3LVHbnK8jg7I5WOtG0kMDbJjbva3l16aS4FmLc5qk0W7fuTWUs4oyFIrXB3kA5Tc355Qpn7FT9DOHmhJhNzx8nzK0EzNTXTtNwVlFkoXIpl__mm4BPGWxwXg-9P9leocOlUTf14Ln15mzeY06pVmVT6xIKHBQmAqoqWk1WZbECkipV49BMtvtuOGI7uqrp3tk6Msa227uYnOLhxKTzRYWsb6-3EIoX3wyhH00Frw50LwKxJ4BwWlo1PA-AK_L7vl92b9M_G_a1_G4s2YDpRw_T0YTtBmIo9qlamlCi5yJyApH2WX5lgCaZ2d8_vaRVa1DTeBVTHtufJ7YYOUEN0ErFekE9pafCMz8sVbG1e-ENbNLKWDxs2y9P8bYHV2mxybJ-XiYwDjQHTbWjv4bHQ2AgELUNsZXC2Ai25ki4iWU3_7liiy6bpeDGw-Fi3CavFDAQZHiMfm3U9PkLILuFh2tLu-ceXtHeuWZtLBEVU1Zj2Y7TRaQgx9EzIphl58hGbFY-_4HZ7zyx4D6kjRZ78YdFThBgVPGi7hpxKjfAZBszUFkAMmWJ_TWUIxxAXBtYcmnEGAeCrRb01qruZPKdVnETSQtG7-cZDTPGBVYqvV26un3n9qQ/74qpjrUuj1Bwy4IK7So95Zdt2I36EvjO/17)I've thought long and hard about the dynamics of this race before jumping in. **
**Minnesota has long been considered simply out of reach for Republicans. The last time it voted for a GOP President was Nixon. The pundits and political experts say a Republican could never win here. **
**But this year is going to be different for three key reasons:**
**FIRST - I'm running against Tim Walz's right hand. All the rampant fraud that took place here happened under Peggy Flanagan's nose as much as it did under his. We will remind voters about that fact every single day. **
**SECOND - The President has increased his voter share each time he has run. He nearly flipped this state in the last election, and Kamala Harris' running mate was our sitting Governor! Momentum is on our side. **
**THIRD - I'm a different kind of candidate, and I know how to win. I didn't reach the top of my industry by pulling my punches. I put in the work. I never backed down. And I kept fighting, no matter what the naysayers said.**
**In my years on the sidelines, I 've seen a LOT of underdogs win football games that the "experts" said were impossible. **
**The Minnesota Senate race will be another chapter in that storied history.

clean_body: - Michele Tafoya here! I never really considered running for office. Ever. I was too focused on raising my family, and being the best football sideline reporter I could be. But even in the last few weeks, Minnesota has become the embodiment of everything wrong with politics today. Tim Walz, his inner circle, and the corrupt political class are too busy: Wasting billions of our tax dollars on fraudulent daycares Failing to condemn the activists storming churches Raising prices on hardworking families Sabotaging anything the President wants to do Forcing men into women's spaces Pitting Americans against each other I can 't sit on the sidelines anymore. That's why I'm throwing my hat in the ring and announcing my candidacy for U.S. Senate! thought long and hard about the dynamics of this race before jumping in. Minnesota has long been considered simply out of reach for Republicans. The last time it voted for a GOP President was Nixon. The pundits and political experts say a Republican could never win here. But this year is going to be different for three key reasons: FIRST - I'm running against Tim Walz's right hand. All the rampant fraud that took place here happened under Peggy Flanagan's nose as much as it did under his. We will remind voters about that fact every single day. SECOND - The President has increased his voter share each time he has run. He nearly flipped this state in the last election, and Kamala Harris' running mate was our sitting Governor! Momentum is on our side. THIRD - I'm a different kind of candidate, and I know how to win. I didn't reach the top of my industry by pulling my punches. I put in the work. I never backed down. And I kept fighting, no matter what the naysayers said. In my years on the sidelines, I 've seen a LOT of underdogs win football games that the "experts" said were impossible. The Minnesota Senate race will be another chapter in that storied history. | | ---|--- Thanks in advance for your support. I'll never forget the good folks who stepped up in the opening days of this campaign. You and I are going to do great things together. I can feel it. Be brave. Do good. - Michele Michele Tafoya Republican for Senate, Minnesota It's official - Michele Tafoya is running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota! Endorse her here:** | Paid for by Tafoya for Senate 3500 Vicksburg LN N, STE 267, Plymouth, MN 55447 |

subject: Folks might’ve killed to have my job…

It was the hardest decision I’ve ever made.
[](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=872a8c36d7f04e80865e860fa1552de4&_e=tQ39A2I2ffDCsBJ4OeDPAe2e2LDmvk95-_Bbtv40mUi2gi9LM91Z3SfRw_m3HdYMxadDwa8B_pjmtmGAlM3PXwN5_6qTv_0XiBmBTfhHhox1kf7PYzNHqzsJahuwM2_GNwhWjj5HwDvOrnWITsPgjKJK8KaKv3-x9PECDmGORJSJmYmEHSbJY_HeKm28xFrjxbXrh21BwazLWCYakc244JDxln2z5B-OYFCh_kyqWGFknUAGUE5dI3W4FMpuw9ByXJCJgG1a5_xwzbfBxOLdrxS9ovCG0Xsk82F769Rf51TGrv6D8VC67tuH8XE3JesAxfPWnzDZbruhVFO5UNUrsx1o_UiIqCJmlpqEuEgiZt2KmWprfKnPKLlDEUcnzjgmnBC-5Uih7uj2NJSx9i2pwkqVXxq9YkrFSzasfMMeOuSL6LpfjRanydMyO_4GDerF3xG65ytfp9hK-SlCkPYdnQ8M6qYwIZBd-Qb0nsiX12PPDyD9R5Lk9EWVFWjkp0SvcX-3g71mmFJcpK39UWqIGycPTQ5kWokaCUYGKZvVLYiaa9TONuVxIa2-lenE_oD3tN_PDwLTDROXHZoySL64o-WOOYOpRF8CMd5YieNBvcgb-ljXlj4WtM-OFPn5Jo1lzaUnQ0RfHI7S1-tCxMTCikkrYvZHZ59a6tmUnPEmw4y5lOeKB_dgj-jgSmBlW497WXFqlF5pVzhj9W3lWHbfxjMxkky96QSgwHkVzVJyMMDSvW5F2L9nf4ZTxJkdlizurioYcW7rV0g_qpoGzUv00aA1XdTO3UccRfUqb_4rOPjS7Whxowl4i-Nki1TjIIZzv7v31rcpuRdcsMJWlCJang%3D%3D)Hi there, I hope you are well!
[This is Michele Tafoya – I’ve got a quick story to share if you’ll indulge me for a few moments.](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=872a8c36d7f04e80865e860fa1552de4&_e=tQ39A2I2ffDCsBJ4OeDPAe2e2LDmvk95-_Bbtv40mUi2gi9LM91Z3SfRw_m3HdYMxadDwa8B_pjmtmGAlM3PXwN5_6qTv_0XiBmBTfhHhox1kf7PYzNHqzsJahuwM2_GNwhWjj5HwDvOrnWITsPgjKJK8KaKv3-x9PECDmGORJSJmYmEHSbJY_HeKm28xFrjxbXrh21BwazLWCYakc244JDxln2z5B-OYFCh_kyqWGFknUAGUE5dI3W4FMpuw9ByXJCJgG1a5_xwzbfBxOLdrxS9ovCG0Xsk82F769Rf51TGrv6D8VC67tuH8XE3JesApnPP2bM78uca4uJcKzRQv17RY8KROMwpt5ue4bdPu2GBN-xhYC-k1pyGH5lG3PdyzKGYAKlAGN1Mi1Tec-Gh7ofBO3ffl-QKPBIBjIiIQ7cVB35LnMhfJ6zgmQV_6_gqvzrbW0zcaw4j8CzSpHdMhd4KQzFOqNcg5Wp2SSQiEKDqbRavAd3FOcQawjMaD4CIOpzacoQWLbi6kEzPwWJJH38E8TifywSDRt4D95AH9lWEFVbLGWJ-zOaYbFiPt9G0Y5Z3Rokei4jce9qjt1XU3w4bXRMD1iuaa8JensVPfJxBRasaiHvNSlSy6r3BakUMruuekE57n_RJiEmYK5sDTIPku6Fgos5l-YUfExpfR5GdJpSpVFfp5-MtwOj1qc-YUbJhqMa0y2_cbT50oSFmIDOas--s7xQZXl-bn5ye2zZAHliGFHIP5Qkx8QygUwwZVcFFOJTugIZYihplsjEzg9aq4PEZYGa8nGc9dBrIzQ2jFpXDNrBTqU8fIbKdh5tySF0EbpOeMsFyX8d6pFXezg%3D%3D)
Folks might've killed to have my job.
I was a sideline reporter for pro football and basketball. I covered some of the most exciting games in history. I watched sports for a living. And I loved every minute, every touchdown, and every Hail Mary.
I reached the pinnacle of my career... but there was one problem:
I had to watch what I said. I was a public-facing representative for a world-class sports network. I couldn't share everything I believed, and no one really knew I was a Conservative.
So I hung up my press badge and quit my dream job so I could speak my mind. [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=872a8c36d7f04e80865e860fa1552de4&_e=tQ39A2I2ffDCsBJ4OeDPAe2e2LDmvk95-_Bbtv40mUi2gi9LM91Z3SfRw_m3HdYMxadDwa8B_pjmtmGAlM3PXwN5_6qTv_0XiBmBTfhHhox1kf7PYzNHqzsJahuwM2_GNwhWjj5HwDvOrnWITsPgjKJK8KaKv3-x9PECDmGORJSJmYmEHSbJY_HeKm28xFrjxbXrh21BwazLWCYakc244JDxln2z5B-OYFCh_kyqWGFknUAGUE5dI3W4FMpuw9ByXJCJgG1a5_xwzbfBxOLdrxS9ovCG0Xsk82F769Rf51TGrv6D8VC67tuH8XE3JesAjHNgmUITJ33LdU-MnlHanAT3qHRqZptpktZe13LLmr852nXuJjHVW0AUvXYI1kICFIMNSGphRn0mVy-IwUu4sQhrybh_twVTtNGd0l7yQptnO4o2wGLRGbFQvDfCmA1axdxhYAOoTpM-rfdXNUg-NWgSRC4fYZXVlWW-FpIisopxo10cpigETVR_953qb5sh6fDpUo96nLvjV4wR2japCP99sQhL1hjCYN83ZtPhRzlZRmdvvxGXONAwNF57o_fEHoNrAuKDr4m31xxpbud-NtTR6zD5bN-G3WKuH_qtgE31qcqbynPdQyLAvk5Z-MgOGoGfjWeIgHwE7fgW_4Vry2h1eIJZ6jIE9sE0_ENd53hKaP7xz29fcWE1DFN1WMjnJn4-ifh2zYFYjYhtbRjAaw_7jnxCOubwkP1WuzIzS3o63R1WMm6e6Oh0JcWGgpmKjMwdyuSkm_X6cVKkY2fBd5LUZye1UPw-D9Wda485m2Sa9a8qcZPq7gZZB-ENr_5hoGdk9sVmhK3Q2UegB_50YA%3D%3D)It was the hardest decision I've ever made. I was scared, excited, and liberated - all at the same time. **
**People said I was OUT OF MY MIND. **
**But I knew God was leading me in a certain direction, and that 's no time for half-measures. I knew he had a plan for my life. So I took a leap of faith and never looked back.**
**[Now, a few years later, I’ve started a growing podcast, campaigned for commonsense in Minnesota, and now launched a campaign for U.S. Senate!](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=872a8c36d7f04e80865e860fa1552de4&_e=tQ39A2I2ffDCsBJ4OeDPAe2e2LDmvk95-_Bbtv40mUi2gi9LM91Z3SfRw_m3HdYMxadDwa8B_pjmtmGAlM3PXwN5_6qTv_0XiBmBTfhHhox1kf7PYzNHqzsJahuwM2_GNwhWjj5HwDvOrnWITsPgjKJK8KaKv3-x9PECDmGORJSJmYmEHSbJY_HeKm28xFrjxbXrh21BwazLWCYakc244JDxln2z5B-OYFCh_kyqWGFknUAGUE5dI3W4FMpuw9ByXJCJgG1a5_xwzbfBxOLdrxS9ovCG0Xsk82F769Rf51TGrv6D8VC67tuH8XE3JesA7uyIaeSDKcHZfBb0mLoRxUosjq-gWAKkpP0dvAS2dadZi-dg0yZges8-3BaiI7V2m7s3nW8sLZbDLtBQDChN-UynfhrXx0KwbuDxH4KEKlXu4JG97q1bxQCDqRweWwaxlum4EP3PBuTgqHypfifJSDP8Lai7HV9WrqTnDTJdS8s8e6SFHRaus3Wgi0dbCNJwZGtDzLjY8advHdq2BOx08bp9D0GLGItGnSynYeQ61xrYBVRHZzElDKpazrHrrui_eESBHroe_VAiVAtpPwoonuRAkeOwvzQvU05bslXWo5DnpGR36mpx9X39-GgykUlm25m-YO5lkXEcd6D9MrsVy1vDkAUc5WoExmJOuttqeHauBG1JSmUnDKnqf-ER-Z2VD9bSMNrvr3saJCQyBQS4T3jSD_eyOgt_ciF3VAsclxcN9GTITc6KB1f9HsahXzHZbOiTSveGLcn3T52RW9SMNBE4ZMdNA1jmNyIv403lKlbEYl4QiwH9MhzL7GV0YYqxtMe0-euTzQI8lozolahz8A%3D%3D)**
**I believe God put me here for a reason. **
**I believe Minnesota is the land of 10,000 problems right now. **
**And I believe a no-BS, sane person who 's ALL-IN can win this seat. **
** But I'd be lying if I said fundraising doesn't make a difference in a race like this. The far-Left is readying a FORTUNE to drown out our message and take this seat out of play. **
**Our path to victory solely depends on raising enough to keep up. **
**My finance team tells me we 'll need to bring in 10,000 founding donors ASAP so we can: 1) Hire staff, 2) Recruit volunteers in every corner of the state, 3) Print yard signs, 4) Launch our first advertising blitz, and 5) Start contacting on-the-fence voters. **
**I 'm afraid that'll make the difference between a winning campaign and a losing one.
[With that said, would you consider becoming one of the 10,000 founding donors now that I have launched my Senate campaign?](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=872a8c36d7f04e80865e860fa1552de4&_e=tQ39A2I2ffDCsBJ4OeDPAe2e2LDmvk95-_Bbtv40mUi2gi9LM91Z3SfRw_m3HdYMxadDwa8B_pjmtmGAlM3PXwN5_6qTv_0XiBmBTfhHhox1kf7PYzNHqzsJahuwM2_GNwhWjj5HwDvOrnWITsPgjKJK8KaKv3-x9PECDmGORJSJmYmEHSbJY_HeKm28xFrjxbXrh21BwazLWCYakc244JDxln2z5B-OYFCh_kyqWGFknUAGUE5dI3W4FMpuw9ByXJCJgG1a5_xwzbfBxOLdrxS9ovCG0Xsk82F769Rf51TGrv6D8VC67tuH8XE3JesAPgjh3eipFHJoi2lea-hCXIpfDz8d5x1awP8caO8CdZk1LLGSPtCLnrhAodubzhj-Ha6uEKECr_JAgIMaOHltSn5uwuOwLFgAI77hwdn5HMJBK73cYkfi8pGnxHYb-K07OECZ267aIYwyBOBz3a-g0gT3hw-Odi3qoKn-Scq3Gvh7af3cgVmY_XhTnidCkSPuvmZBq1TYdCzLjcxrba6e_EY7tze4_4oZ9ugG2Oj-4ntaL4FaUMtROVmQrEpMsIzW4kwCp9QgZ6U3T-2gcZ4UjCgNODK3RqyldkR0f4nTeR83KLSlkDbjjodJDey1WiUF5x8wf71RlJDSXlOTwvULiGs_O0PO7oXXVT_Fzhup8ChkdzArnE6g3vJj0xa-uBhFFWp9s-bThdt0OwQEX-tziu5R8acndnuCUjub4gKiD4GdpTYO8X9Cl1Zu1rxdaoUzVN9da17fRKxx3Z3hn_gVlSkHYr9mpAl-KkeAbfy6kmNXnt3NkNEquast3phgkPTwSGORK25TmE-RQxZAPSCaVg%3D%3D)[BECOME A FOUNDING DONOR
](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=872a8c36d7f04e80865e860fa1552de4&_e=tQ39A2I2ffDCsBJ4OeDPAe2e2LDmvk95-_Bbtv40mUi2gi9LM91Z3SfRw_m3HdYMxadDwa8B_pjmtmGAlM3PXwN5_6qTv_0XiBmBTfhHhox1kf7PYzNHqzsJahuwM2_GNwhWjj5HwDvOrnWITsPgjKJK8KaKv3-x9PECDmGORJSJmYmEHSbJY_HeKm28xFrjxbXrh21BwazLWCYakc244JDxln2z5B-OYFCh_kyqWGFknUAGUE5dI3W4FMpuw9ByXJCJgG1a5_xwzbfBxOLdrxS9ovCG0Xsk82F769Rf51TGrv6D8VC67tuH8XE3JesAooL9Zz3jPM6onft8sSEqeIIhHoCx8v39FVqcFJC8WcTX23r_TPViJ1_Syz85S54vjSUgMkl7UvXwVswyMh2mkBSs88fySdTnpJ6k_vEjpt363eOcQFlf-0BbNszMp3OKbiJGgON9JPHHR1Jk2E744O3ZGl5ShkEeBUpxEeK5gXpsGGgEmav_W5GTDe5cD3Dm5CZ8tYWfPDXG026q0VrwKhA9WrC0pu_jBoGl6ceoTlbly5jRG_XI3Pz_Vs6biwEwsAtuQDJQ_kOZTmdNljVzDtNLw423453dtSQDCz1QjAtZhWZShal_4nXBPAapYzpp6HYj57TuTc_f_PmUHxV-Dwoebb34ssCy5tTanme5W4HXTCmockWHxQBwa5w1ROHkc-nmkc7fLLiMGfk_DMpAzOgSIUQ9nhQjN8d3NKl6gvpWx1zk_McUbbZXiW2kiCGDSzowg-VDHPEt7kanWW-2spz2UpGIFeWHfSmsU-8_LVPM60Vfg8oifT7YkQZ_50KULm7qStvxRuP2pWvOUqpz_A%3D%3D)I put my career on hold to speak out for what I know to be true. **
**I 'm committing my blood, sweat, and tears to take this seat back from the political class. **
**I 'd be honored if you'd be willing to stand alongside me. Donations in these early days make the biggest impact on our path to victory. **
**I hope you 'll join us, but either way, God bless you! **
**Be brave. Do good. - Michele [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=872a8c36d7f04e80865e860fa1552de4&_e=tQ39A2I2ffDCsBJ4OeDPAe2e2LDmvk95-_Bbtv40mUi2gi9LM91Z3SfRw_m3HdYMxadDwa8B_pjmtmGAlM3PXwN5_6qTv_0XiBmBTfhHhox1kf7PYzNHqzsJahuwM2_GNwhWjj5HwDvOrnWITsPgjKJK8KaKv3-x9PECDmGORJSJmYmEHSbJY_HeKm28xFrjxbXrh21BwazLWCYakc244JDxln2z5B-OYFCh_kyqWGFknUAGUE5dI3W4FMpuw9ByXJCJgG1a5_xwzbfBxOLdrxS9ovCG0Xsk82F769Rf51TGrv6D8VC67tuH8XE3JesAX94kTnks7Jn7qb46200O1TpNWhcW1-T7w_A5Tl_Pkud4LzqB76rJlTKD-6RUpK6uTm0_ApAeKC_3Id9t-KGcsvZzNofEeMt2MGLqyTPSxx6Hn4dihDsHYpE_Iy_bFqjG2j39KxTdtg8dUGdJGDjqxmp9igvSpCdKi8-88P1deH_8OtGH5ZdTfnrHph0EC84BHHOyjPLsa756fcdVmr-QTgkODoP_teqEu3p1y8zhR0I4CjSXV6X6DKT8u8n7IZxKhrni-0fw91AjlDUUi4Bjf7TYuh6ktzA5PpuRwNmy_RLvXG_2LzlR2Ws-DunCfydRnVdHF7U4Urlgf-M6fkFR_kx44T9SENHr1vg5O6Hp0V8GfyQhC61CVjpk8WIzv5KCc_3FRgA4cbS2EpldgCHLWJwwKQpjwHfn6tMh2kZ-A4V033mhKM9i1Yplvl64JPgJ47m1scekeSJOAN2uebJQy9N2oEe1J93R_RHJrQqy4NpP-2F0Wk7pvmhGrCYtPA20gNA43gk4S2LMb75hjZ1jcg%3D%3D) Michele TafoyaRepublican for Senate, Minnesota

clean_body: It was the hardest decision I’ve ever made. there, I hope you are well! Folks might've killed to have my job. I was a sideline reporter for pro football and basketball. I covered some of the most exciting games in history. I watched sports for a living. And I loved every minute, every touchdown, and every Hail Mary. I reached the pinnacle of my career... but there was one problem: I had to watch what I said. I was a public-facing representative for a world-class sports network. I couldn't share everything I believed, and no one really knew I was a Conservative. So I hung up my press badge and quit my dream job so I could speak my mind. was the hardest decision I've ever made. I was scared, excited, and liberated - all at the same time. People said I was OUT OF MY MIND. But I knew God was leading me in a certain direction, and that 's no time for half-measures. I knew he had a plan for my life. So I took a leap of faith and never looked back. I believe God put me here for a reason. I believe Minnesota is the land of 10,000 problems right now. And I believe a no-BS, sane person who 's ALL-IN can win this seat. But I'd be lying if I said fundraising doesn't make a difference in a race like this. The far-Left is readying a FORTUNE to drown out our message and take this seat out of play. Our path to victory solely depends on raising enough to keep up. My finance team tells me we 'll need to bring in 10,000 founding donors ASAP so we can: 1) Hire staff, 2) Recruit volunteers in every corner of the state, 3) Print yard signs, 4) Launch our first advertising blitz, and 5) Start contacting on-the-fence voters. I 'm afraid that'll make the difference between a winning campaign and a losing one. put my career on hold to speak out for what I know to be true. I 'm committing my blood, sweat, and tears to take this seat back from the political class. I 'd be honored if you'd be willing to stand alongside me. Donations in these early days make the biggest impact on our path to victory. I hope you 'll join us, but either way, God bless you! Be brave. Do good. - Michele Michele TafoyaRepublican for Senate, Minnesota official - Michele Tafoya is running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota! Endorse her here:**| | Paid for by Tafoya for Senate 3500 Vicksburg LN N, STE 267, Plymouth, MN 55447

name: Angela for Congress
subject: We can send a message with the 2026 elections
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Angela for CA-34
body:
This is our opportunity to put working families first.
# [](https://act.angela4congress.com/go/60?refcode=em20260203-301&t=1&akid=301%2E66908%2EYFpTNE)
Angela Gonzales-Torres is a pro-human rights candidate running to represent CA-34. Email is one of the most important ways that we can keep you informed on this critical race, but if you want to stop receiving emails from us, [**click here**](https://act.angela4congress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=2&akid=301%2E66908%2EYFpTNE).
---
It’s no exaggeration to say that this year’s elections are some of the most important yet. Under Donald Trump’s presidency, they are a chance to _really_ see if we’ll have leaders who will stand up to Trump in Congress.
We have two scenarios moving forward:
❌ Trump and his far-right republicans can continue to consolidate power. Trump can keep republicans AND democrats who will do his bidding in office. We’ll continue to see acts of extraordinary cruelty and hate become the norm. Our rights will continue to be eroded. Human beings will continue to be hunted down and killed. Wars will continue to be started in the name of greed.
✅ We can hold Trump and his entire administration. We can _loudly_ show that Trump doesn’t have the support of the people. We can show leaders in office that we see them and we’re not going to let them stand by while Trump destroys our communities. We can show them that affordability, and putting working families first is a necessity to keep office and resonate with voters.
_Which do you want?_
[**Help us show a strong message that progressive Democratic candidates can win. As voters, we’re over Trump’s tyrannical and cruel rule, and we need real leaders in Congress who will stand up to his violence.**](https://act.angela4congress.com/go/128?c0=em_ms_agt_20260203_1.AIRbmm&amount=5&refcode=em20260203-301&t=3&akid=301%2E66908%2EYFpTNE)

Thank you,
Angela for Congress

clean_body: You STILL haven't taken my MAGA Poll. | | | | I could use a HUGE MAGA hug! You’ve always had my back. Now is NOT the time to quit. I’m going to tell you something straight from the heart: You’re not just a supporter. You’re family. You’re the backbone of this incredible movement. Through the witch hunts, the fake news attacks, the endless hoaxes – you’ve stood by me like nobody else. You’ve got my back, and believe me, that means the world to me. Do you STILL love me? You make me feel like the luckiest guy in America every single day. Your energy, your passion, your incredible loyalty – it’s beautiful. It’s powerful. And honestly? It warms my heart more than anything. So right now, I’m asking you the big question: Do you love me? Do you love what we’re building together? Do you love America enough to keep fighting? If the answer is YES (and I know it is), show me right now with a quick contribution, so we can keep WINNING! Thank you for being the best. I mean it. I love you, Donald J. I’m coming to you with good news: We hit our January fundraising goal, and I couldn't be more grateful!

| Friend, I’m coming to you with good news: We hit our January fundraising goal, and I couldn't be more grateful! The first month of the election year is critical to showing our strength and momentum. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124663044/599305707/-1481197821?amount=10&refcode=02.03.26_og_fr_januarygoal&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNGQxMWEwYS00NDAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6sSHUcSVe9nuoO6ugu_YUXY3RM4Z-l8PFnTiups1kR0=) Your support is the fuel that keeps this campaign moving forward, and it shows that together, we can achieve great things. **But we can't rest just yet.** Every month brings us closer to election day, and the challenges we’re up against are only going to get harder. **Ohio Republicans have redrawn our district, tipping the scales in their favor.** Our goal is clear: We have to maintain this momentum to ensure we can fight back against their schemes. Here's why this matters: I’m committed to reducing costs for Ohio families and making life more affordable. Already this year, I’ve introduced a bipartisan bill to cut prescription drug prices for our seniors. But there's still so much more we can do. Team,

We've won some important victories for Northwest Ohio in the past month, friend! I'll never stop fighting for our home and the community that raised me, because Ohioans deserve nothing less.

Marcy

About Marcy Kaptur
Marcy Kaptur is a fighter for high-wage jobs, growing a workforce to meet the needs of the 21st century, and protecting our region’s most vital natural resource, the Great Lakes. She’s worked with both parties to secure investments in our communities and has been a true friend of veterans, seniors, first responders and working people. She’s a voice for us all in the People’s House.

Fellow Conservative,

As a Republican political candidate running to represent Minnesota in the U.S. Senate, here is what I can tell you from being here during one of the most tumultuous times in recent memory.

1. Democrats believe that they have a reasonably straightforward path to any higher office that they want, simply because our state hasn’t voted for a Republican candidate on the presidential level since 1976

2. The mayor of Minneapolis and Governor Walz like to openly vilify Republicans, believing their supporters would never toss them to the curb simply because they are playing the role of attack dog. The mayor is probably even more of a left-wing ideologue than the governor.

3. They hate President Trump and everything he stands for - that’s why they are not cooperating with ICE in any way, shape, or form.

Now, the mayor, the governor, and my opponent in this Senate race, Lieutenant Governor Peggy Flanagan, are digging in even further, comparing FEDERAL ICE agents to neighbors flooding the city that don’t share the same values as the original inhabitants.

But what about the millions of illegal immigrants who flooded our country that don’t share ANY of our values?

**Democrats in Minnesota are showing the entire country what exactly will happen if they retake the Senate in November or the White House in 2028.

[**READ MYBIO
**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5f1addc49d7548fa8c08e53755ed2727&_e=vhNRjzWtL1KR-OO27V2k2M7vzz8a3Ir_OIzzQ2vekII5Egm2jZPGSsmg_X3NhQYRQBeL5V-2ooRjLKxOr9QD-d432GZSLwKd0KydGTjUBv9c9qSj2pCo7vRhBI4aLCWNgNsyDuLnlym5K0dxECBRKepuHdGlNBSZ86-q7JfAwpmmQ-P3zqAm8h3KK_vfsFU7HUUNIgjj5qUpvt_NS25LzzFVsH2KPPgmxr7sWVKH24Anq0zk4CB7P3NFBz_tccfOAGZ9BPc0oPTbQLP5CRikv9MOUEokFjRNPTpMgjKsrsTcv3LwxaP44pCxkjieiCD9CtQJnHOEqjhhp_EMiniDX96at1uD6WU6jzrIBDau5WcD7z43jin9bfXCcFDSKRGi21pWU_RTiXotW_rBEDXXkJ27HXaOiQIzTinPkZYFCSSMwA4-Cr3AGMTXEYmXLY3mzPVuHJLPTRQcAPeCmnn1Kh0bqkqQWSe8lRQmgLmFyvuE2iuuw1PKfWt7hywr28s30GUStUQSFfgeTx9pZeyOc2PMXVd1XJjtI4L6UbcyC0DZBYEosg1m_UXeHFv0hrMN5pixaoPNxnMYDa6xCtKxI13UtoM6Ole4w4jNCwFZQuP5Vp4RoRzRixzz7IiOtyB1DO1oPZkKy-Aqqtatntr62d8NKDL0wc-xIbKBSfdqKUPF3Tq0aKGmYcdQJqwNULrzQfBVddbH8KkBNrqpRf8yR7-Mz-Avul445MF1gfy_eZU3hFgkA2SGx38sKzWQVMv7f2hUuidCCHHhUJyZMuGWOn1NFodFywPYnJeR_gAu5XQ%3D)
They tolerate flash mobs, violent protests, and they don’t say anything when crazed lunatics storm places of worship, all to prove a point.

But what do you expect from a lieutenant governor who makes a mockery out of herself by cosplaying as a muslim? **

Rather than hand over convicted and suspected criminals to federal authorities, the mayor of Minneapolis and Governor Tim Walz released them into Minnesota communities.

**Somalian immigrants are storming the state capital building.**

_As a former Navy SEAL, I have been trained to rout out corruption, quell violent uprisings, and, most importantly, lead a team with honor._

**[I’m asking you to support my campaign for the United States Senate today because Minnesota needs direction, and Democrats aren't providing any.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5f1addc49d7548fa8c08e53755ed2727&_e=vhNRjzWtL1KR-OO27V2k2M7vzz8a3Ir_OIzzQ2vekII5Egm2jZPGSsmg_X3NhQYRQBeL5V-2ooRjLKxOr9QD-d432GZSLwKd0KydGTjUBv9c9qSj2pCo7vRhBI4aLCWNgNsyDuLnlym5K0dxECBRKepuHdGlNBSZ86-q7JfAwpmmQ-P3zqAm8h3KK_vfsFU7HUUNIgjj5qUpvt_NS25LzzFVsH2KPPgmxr7sWVKH24Anq0zk4CB7P3NFBz_tccfOAGZ9BPc0oPTbQLP5CRikv9MOUEokFjRNPTpMgjKsrsShXQhYV3-YGd2yoH-1AHCsOVBQY57Ziz3pTLyfADhkDDOEZdRrgMG8ft6X_UmF0ueH2Zblx87C0jHpLOfMQjI0XOfZU-kW-AOX8KUZJWXwRbkCQcuPveIFzIuPFLSYDZwN8OrAv7LCdw9BkDKwVr7x70pXfoxzvNuKVNMfGmR3TAOV3qtAgbPrDkwWP9N6ByZR2LTAPbUAV91lcR1XJ7N0TXs-GhYr2Obta9IW2HutjjYT6E2pjw1d558uhrfotauRYReaf8qwhKzJuFC3eig2o87ZRt2FPHtY1nI2LPSfbIB1ejxu1-On4xWdolL1aT9TupJZ4Dq7VhpBx7hIa_pYHp1kEYm3EWvTzw0Rt0sYtuCKdJeXT2JKbJ_PW_tTQ7cT7DhfwByzVOyEKKOADZ-xxAWyXwe0_F2BbCXo5UBesGci5CxrM1dgDmwVLksJ22Ne83hXOoWfBQ7Q9zRkDWCNVeEbAyS-qdZjdKFCnPRjeU3DfahFxux06FoVZtljyHQ%3D) **

**This is a no-fail mission, and Navy SEALS win races. It’s a proven fact. **

SEALS are entrusted with our nation’s most important, covert missions that have national security implications which impact every single American - and I can tell you that as a candidate for political office, I’m ready for this no-fail mission and my opponents are not.

[**CHIP IN $25

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124663683/599307400/48388767?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Newsletter_260203_t1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Newsletter_260203_t1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2Mzc2ZmNiNy00NTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aZwWl9CKSznu5VqBXvynSM8tB8AOzV7Nsi-Nr3Gu94g=) Hey Derek,

Despite the recent snowstorm, Warner HQ has been hard at work getting our message out all across Virginia! Read more to see what we’ve been up to.

[**_But before you do, we have to ask: with so much at stake in this year’s election, would you take the time to donate to Mark’s campaign today? Senator Warner is fighting to keep Virginia blue – and the earlier resources come in, the more strategically we can put them to use._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124663684/599307401/48388767?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Newsletter_260203_t1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Newsletter_260203_t1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2Mzc2ZmNiNy00NTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aZwWl9CKSznu5VqBXvynSM8tB8AOzV7Nsi-Nr3Gu94g=) ******[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124663685/599307402/48388767?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Newsletter_260203_t1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Newsletter_260203_t1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2Mzc2ZmNiNy00NTAxLWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aZwWl9CKSznu5VqBXvynSM8tB8AOzV7Nsi-Nr3Gu94g=)****** **Warner reacts to Tulsi Gabbard and the Fulton County Elections Raid by the FBI** Senator Warner has been sounding the alarms on the FBI’s raid of an election warehouse in Fulton County, Georgia – the center of Trump’s debunked conspiracy theories about the 2020 election.

_And the most shocking part?!_ Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard was seen onsite at the raid. Let’s be clear about this: the Director of National Intelligence is supposed to be a nonpartisan, apolitical role leading our nation’s intelligence community. It has no law enforcement powers and oversees agencies aimed at foreign threats to our country. _There was absolutely no reason for Tulsi Gabbard to be at this raid._

Warner sat down with Jen Psaki on MS NOW to discuss the effect raids like this could have on this year’s midterm elections and just how dangerous it is for officials like Gabbard to indulge in and enable Donald Trump’s 2020 election conspiracy theories. Read more to see what we’ve been up to. = ****=** Warner reacts to Tulsi Gabbard and the Fulton County Elections Raid by the FBI Senator Warner has been sounding the alarms on the FBI’s raid of an election warehouse in Fulton County, Georgia – the center of Trump’s debunked conspiracy theories about the 2020 election. And the most shocking part?! Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard was seen onsite at the raid. Let’s be clear about this: the Director of National Intelligence is supposed to be a nonpartisan, apolitical role leading our nation’s intelligence community. body:

subject: This is the shortest email you’ll get today

body:

This district is ruby red, but Democrats flipped it in a 31-percentage overperformance of 2024. The national fallout over a state legislative special election has been huge. Republicans across the country are frightened that the results in Texas could be repeated across America in November. = | | | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = We already have a record setting number of candidates running for positions from town council all the way to the United States Congress. = Thanks, Indiana Democrats [content goes here] 101 W Washington St., Suite #1110 E Indianapolis, IN 46204 Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. name: Ilhan Omar
subject: Trump’s harassment
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
I’m losing track of the President’s various delusions, lies, harassments, and racist attacks.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=2069%2E791427%2Euq0wNB)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-feb26.5-Kap5&refcode=em260203-1-180&t=2&akid=2069%2E791427%2Euq0wNB)
Friend, I’m losing track of the President’s various delusions, lies, harassments, and racist attacks.
This week, he’s trying to link me to ISIS.
It would be laughable if it weren’t so damaging for our nation and my security.
It’s been only a week since I was attacked at a town hall, and Trump is just continuing to amp up the racist attacks. He is getting even more extreme despite the dramatic increase in death threats I am receiving.
It’s not just dangerous for me, but for all Muslims in America who suffer because their President constantly tells his supporters to fear and hate them.
Think about that — the most powerful man in the world, putting targets on the backs of his own citizens on the basis of their religion and place of birth. It’s the kind of tyranny that America was founded to oppose, and despite our nation’s shortcomings, I believe we can rise above it.
I have seen the resistance to fascism and authoritarianism in my own backyard in Minneapolis. I have seen how regular people of all backgrounds are coming together to organize, to educate, to provide mutual aid, and to protect each other. Not to hate people who are different and scapegoat immigrants.
The bottom line is, I will not give up on building this movement with you, no matter what Trump and MAGA have to say about it. As we press on, **[if you can afford to chip in $10 today, it would help us continue to afford our security team, who keep me and our entire campaign safe as Trump’s attacks inspire more harassment and threats.](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-feb26.5-Kap5&refcode=em260203-1-180&t=3&akid=2069%2E791427%2Euq0wNB)**
**[CHIP IN $10](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-feb26.5-Kap5&refcode=em260203-1-180&t=4&akid=2069%2E791427%2Euq0wNB)**
In solidarity,
Ilhan Omar

Washington D.C., 20033

We must push back against anti-Black racism together. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWFVNqn-KMIohmkrKkxQrPxP1jptjNZ7WnIs9dgu8M9xmq3eXTTkL8W_wpEuySX5Cfgmq-1f6vyzzoZCi3KODgGinafS7yUnjfhwV-JQAhRvyFqx2OQQvW8asXm0nXwc14eArWJ0Ms5lxTBSLWq2YAfjeN4rKtJdOPwcs2WHMyaL-UZBv7Tma_qAU4uh-QsT3KGxIAB3ROxiw1gTSLrsF8rkESF0kO36nVYewiSN36EdCgDwhQtD2Wrt-v9_HPzNjF/4nu/dliyecuySCysIG5jt5YsbA/h0/h001.aXkRZ4Xqm6bribZg8AcpG5SxFvay_UDyRx5bHloyHQM)

Friend, We’re now in Black History Month, and there’s so much we can learn from Black-led movements for liberation that are particularly relevant for these times. Please check out our email below for more information about how our anti-Black policing and prison system intersects with immigration detention, how Black people have resisted state violence and built more resilient communities, and how we can move forward toward a future where all Black people are free and thriving. [**Then, check out this petition demanding Congress move funds away from anti-Black state violence and instead invest our taxpayer dollars in life-saving services and communities’ needs.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38h9nrsCCiMen3CGd5zila9PVaqzmCTkqXSicnFxIeX5QwWMpV3fNkZ2GJw0F-UAe3vV5GEQkO8pg9NqUVlX3m8dv02Zt7PnJ0nweL86F4XM3anDrPTKa-oFSmYAvKJHi3Tilbb9zUVrBww-mAd3BNU97U1qGoU9xcJrZcssE2HIGiiJgEsnZxyezIYPAFtMC6iJBPlq2A3pxULiQfiFdF4LH-VSknIYIr9n2C5nYPpyxqMKG97kRgmkf5uyISQBR9rSndov7OEX0nh12y3nTyfzgAV91ZRpFSB5TEqhmM7O8MVZlZJMBYMALBGFifyj1nN0BBjA5z-RyJmHLhcdNC3-G9dkoFtnCLiKmBQtgecCxPsq_cwnlzk_6QHa_TCp6cP6P6RDE_EH-W0tQuIGhKbkLf-C36l_x6GLxVAaMZQINsikGuR2dstTlthHLbTetJmto0QOShlYolHu-cNiMzNinFwva-esTHXdDsLJgsJjA/4nu/dliyecuySCysIG5jt5YsbA/h1/h001._ITX2juFbHsIMhpm6g01dLvKo9Q6siYMvcWwVHMJ73Y) Thank you, Rashida | [POWER OUR MOVEMENT](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvNYN-Nf8VmRD3J8PbgJvGmtjtBSo0gnrJYj01RAiZP3zqxhB1mR2aEFWffE-wbujQ83IB3M88r1At7umk4_voJasaKnGXVWvpvUsW-ovliSg2mot3IktnULZ3B7gAS41WLEAbc20w2p0mFXibCwKfrnJ8nrjTi7omwAbosXDlFrlS5uZFosAFO9yXM5483DC4MVypX6UHpKmWMelFuGV82YnDdr1tcwldX5TW-1bQ37pj3jVAPKq-XT8oS7Ct47AOurBOiEGg5if0kTjJqKkzCPjoQsZaEvpQbGFAF2JQhycDzpsfO9jHMbTQVbV2MQkviZs0iATIDgjovyKMDdzfIAD5TVKEpx0gXR_8uRbRqX9shzZNUnFtwSM7DU2W3AaNWIfgZ3TI4dZJ8PS_U7Y_YYl2ZCAVXMFnEsOy_qN0OyA/4nu/dliyecuySCysIG5jt5YsbA/h2/h001.wmV63UFi-bDPX1IihRFdX1qowPYPTq6jFmB3ONgQFzQ)

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Rashida Tlaib******
Date: Mon, Feb 2, 2026
Subject: U.S. state-sanctioned violence has always targeted Black people
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Friend,
This Black History Month, let’s shine a light on this country’s state-sanctioned violence that has always targeted Black people.
Don’t forget about Keith Porter, a father who was killed this New Year’s Eve by an off-duty ICE agent. We must Say His Name when talking about the recent victims of ICE violence.
And remember: Trump initially sent federal agents to Minnesota to target Black Somali immigrants.
His administration recently stopped processing green card, asylum, and other immigration applications from people in 19 countries—primarily African and Black-majority countries. And he just revoked Haitians’ Temporary Protected Status, endangering 350,000 U.S. residents.
Last week his administration arrested six Black Minnesotans in connection with a peaceful protest at a church whose pastor works for ICE. Two journalists and four activists were targeted for exercising their First Amendment rights _and_ for the color of their skin.
Trump’s followers also attacked two of my Black colleagues in recent weeks: Reps. Ilhan Omar and Maxwell Frost. Ilhan’s attacker had previously threatened her life, and the person who punched Maxwell said: “We are going to deport you and your kind.”
**The criminalization of immigration is rooted in white supremacist ideology about who belongs here. And it’s intertwined with our country’s anti-Black systems of policing and incarceration, which emerged out of slave patrols.**
No matter which party is in power, Black immigrants in the U.S. have endured racist policing and over-criminalization—as have Black Americans. In Minneapolis alone, police shot Philando Castile in his car and killed George Floyd on the street.
Each year, Congresspeople from both sides of the aisle keep spending more and more of our tax dollars on state violence—such as war abroad and policing and incarceration at home, including immigration enforcement and detention. These systems are all connected.
When the 13th Amendment passed, it abolished slavery—“except as punishment for crime.” Now incarceration is a big business, with for-profit prisons exploiting incarcerated people’s labor _and_ getting big government contracts.
**Black Americans are deeply familiar with state violence.** About a week ago, Jackie Summers wrote in a social media post: _“For Black people, this country has always been fascist. What’s new is who else is inside the blast radius... Study Black history. Our grief, our joy, and how we organized.”_
**For centuries, Black Americans have been leading resistance movements and movements for collective liberation.**
We can look at what happened in response to Fugitive Slave laws, when armed white government agents kidnapped Black people around the country and forced them into slavery.
Everyday people came out en masse to defend their neighbors, blocking arrests and physically getting between them and U.S. Marshals. They formed vigilance committees to alert and show up for their neighbors who were at risk. These committees also helped people in the Underground Railroad, providing passage and shelter.
To overturn Jim Crow laws, Black Americans in the Civil Rights Movement trained each other and coordinated mass boycotts, during which they provided financial and material support for one another.
**Our country has been here before, and we have**** _been_****fighting back. We have blueprints for resisting and for building resilient communities.**
For example, the Black Panthers provided free breakfasts for children, which has since been adopted nationwide in schools. They ran free community survival programs such as health clinics, ambulances, legal aid, education, food pantries, housing cooperatives, and more.
Ruth Wilson Gilmore, a leader in the ongoing movement for abolition, writes that abolition isn’t just about working toward a world with no prisons, it’s about building life-affirming institutions. She explains that _“abolition's goal is to change how we interact with each other and the planet by putting people over profit, welfare over warfare, and life over death.”_
In Congress I’m pushing to move funding away from state violence—including policing and prisons and immigration enforcement and detention centers—and instead invest those taxpayer dollars into our communities' needs.
[**If you agree, please sign our petition demanding Congress support care for our communities over criminalization.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38h9nrsCCiMen3CGd5zila9PVaqzmCTkqXSicnFxIeX5QwWMpV3fNkZ2GJw0F-UAe3vV5GEQkO8pg9NqUVlX3m8dv02Zt7PnJ0nweL86F4XM3anDrPTKa-oFSmYAvKJHi3Tilbb9zUVrBww-mAd3BNU97U1qGoU9xcJrZcssE2HIOGr1jBSQNnvwAFvJecs-eeT5gF5HND2J1BkG_0wvt24aWyFyaEWVNiGrVifihZud6dY4xiXymMuR7pQVVINFmuRdxbdHcLyLbQx0hWP5FDPKWwiTSs3h-RoXQAUBtlu1TAV_1iRtJg1OYDLjaxesfzFzDgCbCJLnYXATdopsPYA6gf0DZpxtHiIDCPHzQjXAW92R5zFRd5ZzPzSjYoQXA0fLc9Dygho2Fxql6-fM-Hsg6_78j7-XNVVYT_y9x4AY59qdsaRKKzhwqmx5Gdis5qpGeQ4tkCyc0g-XcwGJiTSpthan-S0Xxmi5K2npHknHQ/4nu/dliyecuySCysIG5jt5YsbA/h3/h001.XPsaX6Qg1Prw6Jjc6SwDGL6EfYHk7P9YSdQSsEFjTdw)
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38h9nrsCCiMen3CGd5zila9PVaqzmCTkqXSicnFxIeX5QwWMpV3fNkZ2GJw0F-UAe3vV5GEQkO8pg9NqUVlX3m8dv02Zt7PnJ0nweL86F4XM3anDrPTKa-oFSmYAvKJHi3Tilbb9zUVrBww-mAd3BNU97U1qGoU9xcJrZcssE2HIG7T8XiMoSWAlCHfQVGBcLHB7OJsyazJ8OtSy_RI-cn-GtvmOJrwBZLKu8kDEdxd34ndjfexP7P3w_53DpXeJthSKKftVhcJZ5OgycqnENEGFDFnsjIKptSqiwK9lFbftX4BunGrFbKLERDnMsvrcEQN5UgDDvLwCFZYMD7nq5TiCTbFSl38U99Hh7OE587NWo2BJ4TYLPHekh_02CVptCkqOtL3UBQqAo1LodC--HBh1f79nVj54PBqpQu7f3BttNddIoDF3Uc2ny4DUmfJSBm1IigdmxtDPH6yMDKgBOtzfidc9U_qAD7qJCKveqr9oQ/4nu/dliyecuySCysIG5jt5YsbA/h4/h001.CMylDoTQpg1ODZoDpJzTk321aYZdFUny4TZPNg3KTT0)
---
Many transformative policies have already been introduced to get us closer to this reality, and I’ve been proud to co-sponsor many of them:
* The **_People’s Response Act_** would change our country’s approach to public safety by centering care and healing over punishment, including funding non-carceral and unarmed first responders such as mental health and substance use counselors.
* The **_Break the Cycle of Violence Act_** __ would provide needed resources for non-carceral community-based violence intervention programs.
* The **_People’s Justice Guarantee_** would reduce jail and prison populations, eliminate wealth-based discrimination such as pre-trial cash bail, end solitary confinement and forced labor, and so much more.
* The** _Dignity for Detained Migrants Act_** would end mandatory detention, prohibit family and child detention, end private detention facilities and jails, and assert clear standards for treatment of detained immigrants. The **_New Way Forward Act_** has similarities.
* The **_Dismantle Mass Incarceration for Public Health Act_** , which I introduced, would release people in ICE detention, people who haven’t yet been convicted, people who are pregnant, seniors, people nearing the end of their sentences, and more.
* The** _Justice For All Act_** , which I’ve introduced, would end qualified immunity for government employees and strengthen civil rights protections against discrimination—including prohibiting racial profiling in police investigations and activity.
* The **_Melt ICE Act_** would strip funding from detention and surveillance and redirect those resources into health care, housing, and real community safety.

More pieces of legislation are listed on our petition page.
[**Please add your name if you want to see Congress pass some of these transformative policies.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38h9nrsCCiMen3CGd5zila9PVaqzmCTkqXSicnFxIeX5QwWMpV3fNkZ2GJw0F-UAe3vV5GEQkO8pg9NqUVlX3m8dv02Zt7PnJ0nweL86F4XM3anDrPTKa-oFSmYAvKJHi3Tilbb9zUVrBww-mAd3BNU97U1qGoU9xcJrZcssE2HIMPSWtnvQEwLFxNsAEzLL_lU3bneTmJCqFGfK_RYt_T8TT61-8xBmkXI5_tGCItaoxIkWr3epjGu5F2B0ZCnchn2aioCSNm97K7fuvQ-vskzHC8kGmNWJmOm67vL2uIGB7kjVTB0NfRTBRsYeFy2Flg8uT6iD4pF4cuehJvfOb_Dp07EDKbTFwCXvvDuPJE9fEeMlf1BrjD3MpGbT8ZFObxw8etdL38ONz4oaK2I1L30FnrRHq3Oa3QFw69uZ7_SzzjYELpuuT-YRm7L--kglcHpB7UGaS_FQ52rs7LkIzyHJLxBdljKYus5JQyV7x1WQg/4nu/dliyecuySCysIG5jt5YsbA/h5/h001.Z-j73piADdchEX66lllF2QQjYocSFjM5r31VMM4nLOg)
Black movement leaders created Black Futures Month to celebrate not only Black history but also to honor the ongoing work for a liberated future, where all Black people are free and thriving.
Together, we’ll keep working toward decriminalizing immigration and poverty, and moving away from criminalization overall. We’ll keep demanding investments in what actually keeps us safe: making sure everyone has what they need to survive and thrive.
With you in the fight,
Rashida

clean_body: We must push back against anti-Black racism together. | | | Friend, We’re now in Black History Month, and there’s so much we can learn from Black-led movements for liberation that are particularly relevant for these times. Please check out our email below for more information about how our anti-Black policing and prison system intersects with immigration detention, how Black people have resisted state violence and built more resilient communities, and how we can move forward toward a future where all Black people are free and thriving. Thank you, Rashida | \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib** Date: Mon, Feb 2, 2026 Subject: U.S. state-sanctioned violence has always targeted Black people To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, This Black History Month, let’s shine a light on this country’s state-sanctioned violence that has always targeted Black people. Don’t forget about Keith Porter, a father who was killed this New Year’s Eve by an off-duty ICE agent. We must Say His Name when talking about the recent victims of ICE violence. And remember: Trump initially sent federal agents to Minnesota to target Black Somali immigrants. His administration recently stopped processing green card, asylum, and other immigration applications from people in 19 countries—primarily African and Black-majority countries. And he just revoked Haitians’ Temporary Protected Status, endangering 350,000 U.S. residents. Last week his administration arrested six Black Minnesotans in connection with a peaceful protest at a church whose pastor works for ICE. Two journalists and four activists were targeted for exercising their First Amendment rights and for the color of their skin. Trump’s followers also attacked two of my Black colleagues in recent weeks: Reps. Ilhan Omar and Maxwell Frost. Ilhan’s attacker had previously threatened her life, and the person who punched Maxwell said: “We are going to deport you and your kind.” The criminalization of immigration is rooted in white supremacist ideology about who belongs here. And it’s intertwined with our country’s anti-Black systems of policing and incarceration, which emerged out of slave patrols. No matter which party is in power, Black immigrants in the U.S. have endured racist policing and over-criminalization—as have Black Americans. In Minneapolis alone, police shot Philando Castile in his car and killed George Floyd on the street. Each year, Congresspeople from both sides of the aisle keep spending more and more of our tax dollars on state violence—such as war abroad and policing and incarceration at home, including immigration enforcement and detention. These systems are all connected. When the 13th Amendment passed, it abolished slavery—“except as punishment for crime.” Now incarceration is a big business, with for-profit prisons exploiting incarcerated people’s labor and getting big government contracts. Black Americans are deeply familiar with state violence. About a week ago, Jackie Summers wrote in a social media post: “For Black people, this country has always been fascist. What’s new is who else is inside the blast radius... Study Black history. Our grief, our joy, and how we organized.” For centuries, Black Americans have been leading resistance movements and movements for collective liberation. We can look at what happened in response to Fugitive Slave laws, when armed white government agents kidnapped Black people around the country and forced them into slavery. Everyday people came out en masse to defend their neighbors, blocking arrests and physically getting between them and U.S. Marshals. They formed vigilance committees to alert and show up for their neighbors who were at risk. These committees also helped people in the Underground Railroad, providing passage and shelter. To overturn Jim Crow laws, Black Americans in the Civil Rights Movement trained each other and coordinated mass boycotts, during which they provided financial and material support for one another. Our country has been here before, and we have** beenfighting back. We have blueprints for resisting and for building resilient communities. For example, the Black Panthers provided free breakfasts for children, which has since been adopted nationwide in schools. They ran free community survival programs such as health clinics, ambulances, legal aid, education, food pantries, housing cooperatives, and more. Ruth Wilson Gilmore, a leader in the ongoing movement for abolition, writes that abolition isn’t just about working toward a world with no prisons, it’s about building life-affirming institutions. She explains that “abolition's goal is to change how we interact with each other and the planet by putting people over profit, welfare over warfare, and life over death.” In Congress I’m pushing to move funding away from state violence—including policing and prisons and immigration enforcement and detention centers—and instead invest those taxpayer dollars into our communities' needs. Many transformative policies have already been introduced to get us closer to this reality, and I’ve been proud to co-sponsor many of them: The People’s Response Act would change our country’s approach to public safety by centering care and healing over punishment, including funding non-carceral and unarmed first responders such as mental health and substance use counselors. The Break the Cycle of Violence Act would provide needed resources for non-carceral community-based violence intervention programs. The People’s Justice Guarantee would reduce jail and prison populations, eliminate wealth-based discrimination such as pre-trial cash bail, end solitary confinement and forced labor, and so much more. The Dignity for Detained Migrants Act would end mandatory detention, prohibit family and child detention, end private detention facilities and jails, and assert clear standards for treatment of detained immigrants. The New Way Forward Act has similarities. The Dismantle Mass Incarceration for Public Health Act , which I introduced, would release people in ICE detention, people who haven’t yet been convicted, people who are pregnant, seniors, people nearing the end of their sentences, and more. The Justice For All Act , which I’ve introduced, would end qualified immunity for government employees and strengthen civil rights protections against discrimination—including prohibiting racial profiling in police investigations and activity. The Melt ICE Act would strip funding from detention and surveillance and redirect those resources into health care, housing, and real community safety. More pieces of legislation are listed on our petition page. Black movement leaders created Black Futures Month to celebrate not only Black history but also to honor the ongoing work for a liberated future, where all Black people are free and thriving. Together, we’ll keep working toward decriminalizing immigration and poverty, and moving away from criminalization overall. We’ll keep demanding investments in what actually keeps us safe: making sure everyone has what they need to survive and thrive. With you in the fight, Rashida * Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Rashida Tlaib for Congress, please .
